NOTE: Candidates must be based in the Los Angeles Metropolitan area.


We are seeking a Data Analyst to support Zelig, an AI-powered virtual try-on and styling platform redefining how consumers shop. This role is focused on interpreting complex data, including outputs from data models, to understand how system decisions impact business outcomes and to optimize lead-flow and decisioning processes.


This is a highly analytical, business-focused role. While dashboards and visualizations are important, the primary responsibility is data interpretation, decision analysis, and optimization, rather than heavy engineering work.


Zelig is a high-growth company building best-in-class technology, and we are looking for someone who is quick on their feet, naturally curious, and thrives in a collaborative, fast-paced environment.


Key Responsibilities

Data Analysis & Business Impact

  • Interpret complex datasets to understand how system decisions impact business outcomes
  • Tie Zelig recommendations to measurable KPIs such as conversion, funnel progression, and efficiency
  • Identify opportunities to optimize lead flow logic and decision rules to improve performance
  • Translate analytical findings into clear, actionable business recommendations


Data Exploration & Analysis

  • Extract and analyze data from reports and datasets using Excel or Google Sheets
  • Develop a deep understanding of data sources, reporting tools, and inherent limitations
  • Validate assumptions and investigate anomalies across systems and outputs
  • Manage and synthesize multiple datasets across partners, building both internal and client-facing insights


Product & Stakeholder Collaboration

  • Partner closely with Product to define requirements, success metrics, and experimentation frameworks
  • Collaborate on Proofs of Concept (POCs), including stitching and combining data across multiple sources
  • Help document metrics, business logic, and data definitions to ensure consistency and alignment


Enablement & Communication

  • Communicate insights effectively to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
  • Tailor storytelling based on audience (executive vs. operational)
  • Support UX and design partners by providing data context for dashboards and reporting experiences


Team Environment

  • Work cross-functionally with Product, Analytics, and external data partners (including Puller)
  • Collaborate with UX designers to shape intuitive and impactful data visualizations
  • Contribute as a strong team player in a fast-moving, high-growth environment


Must-Have Qualifications

  • Strong working knowledge of SQL. Demonstrated ability to write, review, and optimize complex SQL — including the judgment to evaluate query logic and improve performance.
  • Experience with Tableau and BigQuery (or similar BI, data warehouse tools).
  • 3–5 years of experience in a Product, Data, or Analytics role, preferably within the software or technology sector
  • Strong data literacy with the ability to assess data quality, logic, and business impact
  • Proven ability to translate analysis into actionable recommendations
  • Extensive experience using data to inform product or business decisions
  • Comfortable working with reports, metrics, and exploratory analysis
  • Strong storytelling skills with data and experience presenting insights clearly
  • Ability to adapt communication style for different audiences (executive vs. operational)
  • Experience with experimentation, optimization, or funnel analysis
  • Exposure to data science-driven or rules-based decisioning systems
  • Ability to manage and synthesize multiple datasets across internal and external partners
  • Experience working in or alongside AI-driven products is a strong plus


What We’re Looking For

  • Naturally curious with a strong desire to understand the “why” behind the data
  • Quick thinker who can operate effectively in a fast-paced, evolving environment
  • Strong collaborator who works well across teams and disciplines
  • Someone that can work in the office 4-5 days a week


Compensation & Benefits

  • $90,000 - $120,000 base salary depending on experience
  • Flexible healthcare coverage with multiple medical plan options, including HSA-compatible plans
  • Full suite of benefits: dental, vision, life, and disability insurance (employer-paid options available)
  • Tax-advantaged savings accounts (HSA, FSA, dependent care) to support your financial wellbeing
  • 401(k) retirement plan with robust investment choices
  • Employee Assistance Program (EAP) and concierge health advocacy services for you and your family
  • Lifestyle perks including commuter benefits, wellness programs, pet care support, and exclusive discounts

Title: Data Analyst I
Description:
Stackline is the first full-funnel connected commerce platform for the world's most innovative brands. Business leaders, product innovators, performance marketers, and analysts trust Stackline as the single source of commerce truth. Fueled by our proprietary neural network, our market insights, revenue metrics, behavior analyses, and autonomous functionality create the actions that determine success or failure.

Founded in 2014 in Seattle, we have offices in Minneapolis, New York, Salt Lake City, and London. Since November 2020, Stackline has raised $180 million in strategic investments from Goldman Sachs Growth Equity and TA Associates.

Stackline is on a mission to fuel the future of commerce by bringing brands and customers closer together.

About the Role:

Join us as a Data Analyst where you will step into a dynamic role that that makes a real impact for our clients. If you are passionate about uncovering hidden insights within complex data sets and transforming raw data into actionable knowledge, then we want to talk with you! In this role you will embark on an adventure that utilized your analytical prowess to decode data, identify trends, and help shape the future of Stackline. Apply today and help us on our quest to unlock the full potential of data while contributing to meaningful change! This is a hybrid role (4 days/week in office) and is based out of our Seattle, WA office.

What You Will Do:


Conduct comprehensive dataset analyses across Stackline to extract insights, trends, and patterns utilizing mathematical, statistical, and probability methodologies.
Build and maintain data pipelines and workflows to ensure data accuracy and consistency, while identifying and rectifying errors, inconsistencies, or missing information.
Provide timely and precise responses to inquiries from both internal and external sources regarding datasets and models.
Apply technical expertise to process complex datasets, establish quality control procedures, and derive actionable insights through analytics.
Develop scalable processes for automating reports and visualizations to communicate findings effectively to stakeholders.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand data requirements and address specific product questions or challenges.
Document data sources and processes for data analysis.


Who We Are Looking For:


Bachelor’s degree in Mathematics, Physics, Computer Science, or other relevant technical field.
2+ years of direct industry work experience in one or more of the following areas: data science, data analytics, or data engineering.
2+ years of experience with statistics and probability, particularly their application in systems analysis and operations research.
Demonstrated experience with Python and SQL.


Bonus Points If You Have:


Prior experience using advanced statistical tools such as SciPy or sklearn.
Prior experience with big data technologies such as Hadoop or Spark.
Demonstrated experience designing and building innovative ideas, working closely with technical teams from concept generation through implementation.
Experience working in a startup, retail, digital advertising, or e-commerce environment.


Benefits And Perks

It's important that each and every employee feels they are supported and can complete their life's best work today and in the future. As part of that, we are committed to doing our part in addressing pay gaps and discrepancies by providing pay transparency for all of our roles. Actual salaries are just one component of the compensation package and may vary above or below the range based on job-related knowledge, skills, experience, location, and performance. The pay range for this position in Seattle is $85,000 - $105,000 per year. Other rewards may include annual bonuses, short- and long-term incentives, and other team-specific awards. In addition we provide a robust benefits and perks package that includes:


Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage that actually supports you — including HSA with company match and FSA options
Fertility benefits to support your path to parenthood
401(k) with company match to help you plan ahead
Company-paid life insurance
20 days of PTO + 9 company holidays to truly unplug
Paid parental leave for all parents — because family matters
Summer Fridays (log off at 3pm and enjoy it)
Regular in-office social events including happy hours and catered lunches
A thoughtfully stocked kitchen with healthy snacks and fresh fruit


Stackline is committed to creating a diverse environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We encourage applicants from all backgrounds to apply. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Analytica is seeking a Data Analyst with strengths in data science and exposure to emerging AI capabilities (preferred) to support enterprise programs within the Defense Health Agency (DHA), aligned to the Measures Team in the Chief Data Analytics Office (CDAO).  

This role combines management consulting and advanced analytics to develop and operationalize clinical and business performance measures across the Military Health System (MHS). The position focuses on transforming complex data into actionable insights, performance metrics, and decision-support products that improve healthcare quality, readiness, and operational efficiency.  

Analytica has been recognized by Inc. Magazine as one of the fastest-growing 250 businesses in the US for 3 years. We work with U.S. government clients in health, civilian, and national security missions to build better technology products that impact our day-to-day lives. The company offers competitive compensation with opportunities for bonuses, employer-paid health care, training and development funds, and 401k match.    


Responsibilities: ​ 

Develop and maintain clinical and business performance measures aligned with DHA/MHS priorities  

Maintain and update assigned measures within the centralized Measure Library, ensuring consistency and adherence to governance standards  

Define measure specifications, methodologies, and calculation logic  

Identify appropriate data sources, benchmarks, and thresholds using evidence-based practices  

Analyze performance metrics to identify trends and improvement opportunities  

Produce process and outcome measures and support leadership reporting needs  

Support development and refinement of performance measures and analytics outputs to inform decision-making  

Apply data science techniques to analyze data and support predictive insights  

Identify opportunities to incorporate AI/ML capabilities where applicable  

Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders and support working groups  

Prepare briefings, reports, and presentations for leadership  

Provide guidance on metrics interpretation and analytics outputs  

Engage with stakeholders to capture requirements and define new or updated performance measures  

Research and evaluate data sources; design methodologies for measure calculation and validation  

Author and maintain Interface Control Documents (ICDs) and detailed specifications; obtain stakeholder approval and coordinate handoff to Data Operations for implementation  

Update and govern measure artifacts within the Measure Library to support transition to Data-as-a-Product frameworks  
 

Required Qualifications: ​ 

Bachelor’s degree in Business, Healthcare, Data Analytics, Computer Science, Statistics, or related field  

5+ years of experience in management analysis, analytics, or related roles  

Experience working with KPIs, performance measures, or metrics frameworks  

Strong ability to synthesize complex data into actionable insights and recommendations  

​Must possess or be eligible for a SECRET clearance 

Preferred: 

Experience supporting DHA, MHS, or federal healthcare environments  

Knowledge of measure development, healthcare quality metrics, or performance management  

Proficiency with Python, SAS, SQL, Git (or similar analytics & version control tools)  

Exposure to AI/ML or advanced analytics solutions  

About Analytica: Analytica is a leading consulting and information technology solutions provider to public sector organizations supporting health, civilian, and national security missions. The company is an award-winning SBA certified small business that has been recognized by Inc. Magazine each of the past three years as one of the 250 fastest-growing companies in the U.S. Analytica specializes in providing software and systems engineering, information management, analytics & visualization, agile project management, and management consulting services. The company is appraised by the Software Engineering Institute (SEI) at CMMI® Maturity Level 3 and is an ISO 9001:2008 certified provider.

Title: Operations Data Analyst
Description:
POSITION PURPOSE: Old World Industries is seeking a Operations Data Analyst to support data-driven decision-making across supply chain operations. Reporting to the Senior Director of Planning, this role will focus on delivering accurate, timely reporting and actionable insights across areas such as demand planning, supply planning, logistics, and procurement.

This position will partner closely with Supply Chain, Business Operations, and IT to enhance data visibility, improve reporting consistency, and support ongoing analytics initiatives. The role will also contribute to the exploration and adoption of emerging analytics capabilities, including AI and automation, to strengthen supply chain performance over time.

Duties, Tasks And Responsibilities

Reporting & Analytics (Primary Focus)


Develop, maintain, and enhance dashboards and reports (primarily in Power BI) to support Supply Chain, Logistics, and Purchasing teams.
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and reliability across reporting outputs.
Translate business requirements into clear, actionable reporting and analytics solutions.
Support the creation and maintenance of standardized KPI reporting for supply chain performance (e.g., forecast accuracy, inventory levels, service levels, transportation metrics).
Provide ongoing reporting and analytical support for planning cycles, including demand and supply planning.


Data Management & Governance (Support Role)


Partner with IT and data teams to support data governance, data quality, and data consistency initiatives.
Assist in identifying and resolving data integrity issues impacting reporting and decision-making.
Contribute to maintaining a “single source of truth” for key supply chain data elements.
Support documentation of data definitions, reporting logic, and KPI standards.


Business Partnership


Work closely with Supply Chain stakeholders to understand reporting needs and identify opportunities for improved insights.
Support ad hoc analysis to identify trends, inefficiencies, and opportunities for cost savings or service improvements.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams (Planning, Logistics, Procurement, Finance) to align reporting and metrics.


Advanced Analytics & Continuous Improvement


Support development of analytical models for forecasting, inventory optimization, and operational performance tracking.
Assist in analyzing demand patterns, supply variability, and logistics performance to inform business decisions.
Identify opportunities to automate manual reporting processes and improve efficiency.


Technology & AI Enablement (In Partnership with IT)


Identify and implement AI/ML use cases (e.g., forecasting, segmentation, anomaly detection)
Collaborate with data science teams to deploy predictive and prescriptive models
Evaluate and integrate AI tools to automate reporting and operational workflows
Develop data pipelines and workflows that support AI-driven decision-making (i.e., Power Automate, Python…)


Skills, Knowledge And Abilities


Bachelor’s Degree in Business, Supply Chain, Data Analytics, Information Systems, or related field.
5–8+ years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, or supply chain analytics.
Strong experience with Power BI, including dashboard development and data visualization best practices.
Expertise in data analysis tools (e.g., Excel, SQL, Tableau, Python)
Understanding of supply chain processes, including demand planning, inventory management, logistics, and procurement.
Familiarity with data governance and data quality concepts.
Ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights for business users.
Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
Effective communication and collaboration skills; ability to work cross-functionally.
Intellectual curiosity and interest in leveraging data, analytics, and emerging technologies (including AI) to improve business performance


This Opportunity Offers a Competitive Benefits Package Including


Medical, dental, and vision coverage with wellness benefits
Company-matching 401(k) plan
Company-paid life insurance and accidental death & dismemberment benefits
Company-paid identity theft insurance
Company-paid disability
Flexible spending and health savings accounts
Company-paid employee assistance program
Paid time off and paid holidays
Additional benefits included: voluntary life insurance, legal benefits, critical illness, accident insurance, hospital indemnity insurance, and pet insurance


Old World Industries is among the largest privately held companies competing in the automotive aftermarket today. Built upon a 50-year history of ingenuity, the Northbrook, Illinois-based company is transforming the automotive, commercial and chemical industries that keep the world moving forward. Old World’s brands include a full line of PEAK® Performance Automotive Aftermarket Products including Antifreeze/Coolant, Washer Fluid, Lighting and Wiper Blades; BlueDEF® Diesel Exhaust Fluid and Equipment; FINAL CHARGE® PRO-SERIES Heavy Duty Extended Life Coolant, Fleet Charge® SCA Precharged Heavy Duty Coolant and Thermal Charge® Heat Transfer Fluid.

POSITION PURPOSE: Old World Industries is seeking a Operations Data Analyst to support data-driven decision-making across supply chain operations. Reporting to the Senior Director of Planning, this role will focus on delivering accurate, timely reporting and actionable insights across areas such as demand planning, supply planning, logistics, and procurement.

This position will partner closely with Supply Chain, Business Operations, and IT to enhance data visibility, improve reporting consistency, and support ongoing analytics initiatives. The role will also contribute to the exploration and adoption of emerging analytics capabilities, including AI and automation, to strengthen supply chain performance over time.

Duties, Tasks And Responsibilities

Reporting & Analytics (Primary Focus)

  • Develop, maintain, and enhance dashboards and reports (primarily in Power BI) to support Supply Chain, Logistics, and Purchasing teams.
  • Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and reliability across reporting outputs.
  • Translate business requirements into clear, actionable reporting and analytics solutions.
  • Support the creation and maintenance of standardized KPI reporting for supply chain performance (e.g., forecast accuracy, inventory levels, service levels, transportation metrics).
  • Provide ongoing reporting and analytical support for planning cycles, including demand and supply planning.

Data Management & Governance (Support Role)

  • Partner with IT and data teams to support data governance, data quality, and data consistency initiatives.
  • Assist in identifying and resolving data integrity issues impacting reporting and decision-making.
  • Contribute to maintaining a “single source of truth” for key supply chain data elements.
  • Support documentation of data definitions, reporting logic, and KPI standards.

Business Partnership

  • Work closely with Supply Chain stakeholders to understand reporting needs and identify opportunities for improved insights.
  • Support ad hoc analysis to identify trends, inefficiencies, and opportunities for cost savings or service improvements.
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams (Planning, Logistics, Procurement, Finance) to align reporting and metrics.

Advanced Analytics & Continuous Improvement

  • Support development of analytical models for forecasting, inventory optimization, and operational performance tracking.
  • Assist in analyzing demand patterns, supply variability, and logistics performance to inform business decisions.
  • Identify opportunities to automate manual reporting processes and improve efficiency.

Technology & AI Enablement (In Partnership with IT)

  • Identify and implement AI/ML use cases (e.g., forecasting, segmentation, anomaly detection)
  • Collaborate with data science teams to deploy predictive and prescriptive models
  • Evaluate and integrate AI tools to automate reporting and operational workflows
  • Develop data pipelines and workflows that support AI-driven decision-making (i.e., Power Automate, Python…)

Skills, Knowledge And Abilities

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Business, Supply Chain, Data Analytics, Information Systems, or related field.
  • 5–8+ years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, or supply chain analytics.
  • Strong experience with Power BI, including dashboard development and data visualization best practices.
  • Expertise in data analysis tools (e.g., Excel, SQL, Tableau, Python)
  • Understanding of supply chain processes, including demand planning, inventory management, logistics, and procurement.
  • Familiarity with data governance and data quality concepts.
  • Ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights for business users.
  • Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
  • Effective communication and collaboration skills; ability to work cross-functionally.
  • Intellectual curiosity and interest in leveraging data, analytics, and emerging technologies (including AI) to improve business performance

This Opportunity Offers a Competitive Benefits Package Including

  • Medical, dental, and vision coverage with wellness benefits
  • Company-matching 401(k) plan
  • Company-paid life insurance and accidental death & dismemberment benefits
  • Company-paid identity theft insurance
  • Company-paid disability
  • Flexible spending and health savings accounts
  • Company-paid employee assistance program
  • Paid time off and paid holidays
  • Additional benefits included: voluntary life insurance, legal benefits, critical illness, accident insurance, hospital indemnity insurance, and pet insurance

Old World Industries is among the largest privately held companies competing in the automotive aftermarket today. Built upon a 50-year history of ingenuity, the Northbrook, Illinois-based company is transforming the automotive, commercial and chemical industries that keep the world moving forward. Old World’s brands include a full line of PEAK® Performance Automotive Aftermarket Products including Antifreeze/Coolant, Washer Fluid, Lighting and Wiper Blades; BlueDEF® Diesel Exhaust Fluid and Equipment; FINAL CHARGE® PRO-SERIES Heavy Duty Extended Life Coolant, Fleet Charge® SCA Precharged Heavy Duty Coolant and Thermal Charge® Heat Transfer Fluid.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
We’re building a world of health around every individual — shaping a more connected, convenient and compassionate health experience. At CVS Health®, you’ll be surrounded by passionate colleagues who care deeply, innovate with purpose, hold ourselves accountable and prioritize safety and quality in everything we do. Join us and be part of something bigger – helping to simplify health care one person, one family and one community at a time.

Position Summary

We're seeking a Data Engineer to design and implement data pipelines that power analytical capabilities. This hands-on role requires an understanding of data engineering best practices and the ability to translate business requirements into technical solutions.

Responsibilities

You will be part of a dedicated team creating datasets for analytic and data science workloads. You will work with other Data Engineers and Sr. Data Engineers on designing, implementing, and testing data pipelines. Your key responsibilities are:


Data Pipeline Development: Design and build ETL/ELT data pipelines to ingest, process, and transform datasets from multiple sources.
Performance Optimization: Implement best practices for performance tuning, partitioning, and clustering to optimize data queries.
Data Quality & Governance: follow data quality standards, data governance frameworks, and security policies for data storage and access.
Data Modeling & Architecture: Develop and optimize data models and schemas to support analytics, reporting, and machine learning requirements.
Data Integration & Transformation: Collaborate with data scientists and analysts to design data solutions that integrate with BI tools and machine learning models
Documentation & Knowledge Sharing: Create comprehensive documentation for data pipelines, workflows, and processes.


Required Qualifications


2+ years of applicable work experience
Proficiency in Python, specifically with ETL pipelines
Strong proficiency in SQL and experience in developing complex queries
Experience deploying data pipelines in a cloud environment (any of Azure, AWS, GCP)
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively with data scientists and analysts.


Preferred Qualifications


Experience working with healthcare data, especially Epic.
Experience using GCP’s BigQuery
Knowledge of data governance best practices in a cloud environment.
Experience working with Machine Learning processes.


Education

College degree or certification in related fields

Anticipated Weekly Hours

40

Time Type

Full time

Pay Range

The Typical Pay Range For This Role Is

$64,890.00 - $173,040.00

This pay range represents the base hourly rate or base annual full-time salary for all positions in the job grade within which this position falls. The actual base salary offer will depend on a variety of factors including experience, education, geography and other relevant factors. This position is eligible for a CVS Health bonus, commission or short-term incentive program in addition to the base pay range listed above.

Our people fuel our future. Our teams reflect the customers, patients, members and communities we serve and we are committed to fostering a workplace where every colleague feels valued and that they belong.

Great Benefits For Great People

We take pride in offering a comprehensive and competitive mix of pay and benefits that reflects our commitment to our colleagues and their families.

This full‑time position is eligible for a comprehensive benefits package designed to support the physical, emotional, and financial well‑being of colleagues and their families. The benefits for this position include medical, dental, and vision coverage, paid time off, retirement savings options, wellness programs, and other resources, based on eligibility.

Additional details about available benefits are provided during the application process and on Benefits Moments.

We anticipate the application window for this opening will close on: 06/05/2026

Qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with all federal, state and local laws.

Title: Sr Data Analyst- Remote
Description:
What you will do:

As the Senior Analyst, Sales Analytics, you will drive the development of the Sales Information and Analytics teams to be able to better analyze field sales performance trends, field orders and related sales issues, including individual and branch/agency results as requested by field management and as required by Finance and Senior Leadership. You will identify trends and recommend actions to support/eliminate those trends. You will also support the development, enhancement and maintenance of team resources, systems and processes. This role is onsite however we are open to fully remote for exceptional candidates.


Leverage technical understanding and business acumen to translate high-level business requirements into technical and functional specifications.
Drive the development plan of Microsoft Power BI to educate, create and deliver actionable dashboards. Create and deliver executive sales dashboards for monthly review by Senior Sales Management.
Hold and lead regular meetings with our IS partners to become the subject matter expert on our Azure system and ensure data accuracy.
Collaborate with internal business partners to support their development of Power BI reports.
Support annual national sales and provide materials for further learning and marketing of our team
Calculate monthly field sales performance and facilitate Senior Sales Management recognition tools.
Hold quarterly audits of current processes; identify and implement methods to improve data accuracy and ease of report use.


Required

What you need


Bachelor's degree
2+ years experience in sales operations or data analytics


Preferred


Experience in Power BI


Posted Date: 05/15/2026 This role will be posted for a minimum of 3 days.

United States of America Pay Ranges:


USN: $77,700 - $129,500 USD Annual
US5: $81,600 - $136,000 USD Annual
US10: $85,500 - $142,500 USD Annual
US15: $89,400 - $148,900 USD Annual
US20: $93,200 - $155,400 USD Annual
US30: $101,000 - $168,400 USD Annual


View the U.S. work location and transparency guide to find the pay range for your location.

Health benefits include: Medical and prescription drug insurance, dental insurance, vision insurance, critical illness insurance, accident insurance, hospital indemnity insurance, personalized healthcare support, wellbeing program and tobacco cessation program. Financial benefits include: Health Savings Account (HSA), Flexible Spending Accounts (FSAs), 401(k) plan, Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP), basic life and AD&D insurance, and short-term disability insurance. Stryker offers innovative products and services in MedSurg, Neurotechnology, Orthopaedics and Spine that help improve patient and healthcare outcomes. Alongside its customers around the world, Stryker impacts more than 150 million patients annually. Depending on customer requirements employees and new hires in sales and field roles that require access to customer accounts as a function of the job may be required to obtain various vaccinations as an essential function of their role.

Title: Ai Data Reviewer - Remote
Description:
Job Title: AI Data Reviewer

Job Type: Contractor

Location: Remote

As an AI Data Reviewer, you'll apply your expertise to help train next-generation AI systems. Your work will shape how models learn, reason, and perform through high-quality, real-world input. No prior experience in AI is required — your domain knowledge is what matters.

Key Responsibilities


Review and analyze screenshots of diverse software interfaces, identifying UI elements and interpreting user workflows with precision.
Evaluate and annotate AI model responses, ensuring judgments are strictly grounded in what is visible on the screen.
Distinguish between observable evidence and assumptions, documenting clear, well-reasoned explanations for your assessments.
Complete structured annotation and data review tasks across dashboards, forms, admin panels, CRM tools, and various web applications.
Collaborate with the customer’s team to provide high-quality, actionable feedback that directly impacts AI model improvements.
Maintain rigorous attention to detail, adhering to established guidelines and quality standards in all deliverables.
Communicate insights and findings effectively, both in writing and verbally, to contribute to team knowledge-sharing and continuous improvement.


Required Skills And Qualifications


Expertise in screenshot analysis and UI interpretation across multiple software environments.
Exceptional attention to detail, with a commitment to accuracy and consistency in data review tasks.
Outstanding written communication skills and a strong ability to articulate complex observations concisely.
High computer literacy and comfort navigating a variety of digital tools, platforms, and workflows.
Demonstrated ability to distinguish between evidence-based findings and assumptions.
Experience completing structured annotation or review processes in a technical, product, or operational setting.
Ability to prioritize, manage time effectively, and meet deadlines in a remote work environment.


Preferred Qualifications


Background in QA testing, technical support, product operations, UX research, data annotation, or technical writing.
Familiarity with reviewing complex interfaces such as admin panels, CRM tools, and modern web apps.
Prior experience working remotely with globally distributed teams.


Skills: software,writing,data review,web,annotation

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Job Description:

Design, develop, and maintain scalable data pipelines and architectures to support analytics and business intelligence initiatives.

Integrate data from multiple sources, ensuring consistency, reliability, and data quality.

Collaborate with data analysts, scientists, and other stakeholders to define data requirements and optimize data workflows.

Implement ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) processes for structured and unstructured data.

Monitor and troubleshoot data systems to ensure performance, security, and efficiency.

Document data processes, system configurations, and best practices.

Requirements:

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Engineering, Information Systems, or related field.

Proven experience with SQL, Python, and data pipeline frameworks.

Strong understanding of data warehousing concepts and cloud platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud).

Experience with big data technologies such as Spark, Hadoop, or Kafka is an advantage.

Excellent problem-solving, analytical, and communication skills.

Ability to work both independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment.

Benefits:

Competitive salary and performance-based incentives.

Opportunities for professional development and technical training.

Innovative and collaborative work culture.

Flexible or hybrid working arrangements.

The organization operates within the remote work staffing space, focusing on providing flexible work-from-home opportunities across the United States. It addresses the need for adaptable employment options that allow individuals to balance professional responsibilities with personal and family commitments. The program supports a variety of industries by connecting candidates with roles that suit their typing and data entry skills, enabling remote productivity and inclusion.




2. About the Opportunity:

The Administrative Assistant / Data Entry Clerk role offers a remote, work-from-home position focused on accurate and efficient data entry and typing tasks. This role is essential for maintaining organized and timely information processing. It requires dependable individuals who can work independently in a distraction-free environment and contribute to the organization''s operational needs by handling data with precision and consistency.

• Complete data entry tasks with accuracy and speed

• Use phone devices, laptops, or computers to perform work

• Maintain focus and productivity in a remote work setting

• Minimum typing speed of 30 words per minute

• Reliable internet connection

• Proficiency with basic PC skills

• Ability to write and speak basic English

• Self-motivated and able to work without distraction

Title: Administrative Assistant / Data Entry Clerk (Remote Work At Home)
Description:
About Our Client

The organization is a dedicated provider of remote work solutions, specializing in accessible work-from-home opportunities within the data entry and administrative sectors. By offering flexible roles that accommodate diverse backgrounds and skill levels, the company addresses the growing demand for employment that balances professional productivity with personal and family commitments. Their program serves as a vital entry point for individuals across the United States seeking a stable, remote career path.







About the Opportunity

The Administrative Assistant / Data Entry Clerk is a foundational remote position focused on high-accuracy information processing and administrative support. Operating from your own home office, you will be responsible for ensuring that organizational data is handled efficiently and communication channels remain clear. This role is ideal for self-motivated individuals who take pride in working independently and maintaining high standards of reliability without direct in-person supervision.







Responsibilities

Data Entry: Perform high-volume, accurate data entry into internal systems.
Productivity: Maintain a consistent typing speed of at least 30 words per minute while ensuring zero errors.
Focus: Demonstrate the ability to complete repetitive tasks with a high degree of focus and minimal distraction.
Communication: Coordinate with clients and internal team members via email to resolve inquiries or provide updates.
Equipment Management: Utilize personal devices (laptop, computer, or phone) to access company portals and complete daily work queues.







Requirements

Age & Residency: Must be at least 16 years old and a current resident of the United States.
Technical Skills: Proficient with basic PC functions and a stable, high-speed internet connection.
Language: Basic proficiency in English writing and speaking.
Environment: Must have a dedicated workspace conducive to independent work without external distractions.
Hardware: Access to a computer, laptop, or compatible mobile device for task completion.




Compensation & Benefits

Compensation

Hourly Rate: $16.00 – $30.00 per hour.
Note: Final pay is determined by your prior experience and your ability to demonstrate an office-equivalent level of productivity while working remotely.







Benefits

Maximum Flexibility: Complete your tasks on a schedule that fits your life.
Remote-First Culture: Skip the commute and work from the comfort of your own home.
Skill Development: Gain experience in administrative operations and digital data management within a professional context.

Title: Data Analytics Consultant
Description:
We are looking for a Data Analytics Consultant to help organizations shape and deliver modern data solutions that turn complex information into actionable business insight. This role partners with stakeholders across technical and executive teams to define data strategies, recommend scalable platforms, and guide implementation across cloud-based environments. Based in Wyomissing, Pennsylvania, the position combines advisory expertise with hands-on architecture and analytics leadership to support impactful client outcomes.

Responsibilities:
• Partner with clients as a technical advisor, helping them evaluate options and make informed decisions on analytics and data platform solutions.
• Architect and deliver data environments across public and private cloud ecosystems, ensuring solutions are scalable, secure, and aligned with business goals.
• Lead discussions on data governance, quality standards, architecture direction, and long-term analytics strategy for client organizations.
• Facilitate discovery meetings, workshops, and executive presentations to gather requirements and translate business needs into practical technical plans.
• Explain emerging data technologies and analytics capabilities in a way that highlights measurable value for both business and technical audiences.
• Establish effective approaches for collecting, storing, transforming, and analyzing data to support reporting, operational insight, and decision-making.
• Create implementation roadmaps and phased delivery plans for analytics initiatives, including assessments of current-state environments and future-state architecture.
• Review existing data solutions, identify improvement opportunities, and produce clear documentation that supports design decisions and project execution.
• Collaborate with stakeholders ranging from senior executives to engineering teams while contributing across multiple phases of the project lifecycle, including pre-sales support.
• Apply analytics expertise to use cases involving risk monitoring, fraud-related analysis, and investigative data interpretation where business needs require it.
• Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related technical field.
• At least 5 years of experience delivering Microsoft-based reporting, analytics, visualization, and data integration solutions.
• Minimum of 5 years of hands-on experience designing and building data platforms, data pipelines, and analytics solutions.
• Deep expertise with Microsoft Fabric workloads, including Power BI reporting, Data Factory, Data Engineering, Data Science, Data Warehouse, Database, and Real-Time Intelligence.
• Strong knowledge of relational and NoSQL data platforms, modern data lake and lakehouse concepts, and enterprise data architecture patterns.
• Demonstrated ability to communicate effectively with executive leadership, business stakeholders, and technical teams in a client-facing environment.
• Proficiency with Python and generative AI tools for development, along with experience implementing security features such as row-level security.
• Active Microsoft Fabric certification required; experience with technologies such as Databricks, Snowflake, Azure Synapse Analytics, Azure Data Factory, stream analytics, and dimensional modeling approaches is a plus.
Technology Doesn't Change the World, People Do.®

Title: Web Data Analyst (Mid–Senior Level)
Description:
Benefits:


Dental insurance
Health insurance
Paid time off
Training & development
Vision insurance



Rysun Labs (formerly KCS – Krish Compusoft Services) is an AI, Data & Digital innovation partner of choice for enterprises, globally. Rysun guides and accelerates the Data & AI strategy and Digital Transformation programs for Fortune 2000 enterprises and product startups to shape remarkable customer experiences and intelligent operations. The team delivers innovative, specialized solutions that help High-tech, Retail & Ecommerce to outperform competition and lead the change in their industry. Rysun partners with Microsoft, Google and AWS to bring the best of enterprise technology to its customers. Rysun believes in quality-first and is CMMI Level 5, ISO 9001 & 27001 certified. The team has a growth mindset fueled by a strong culture of collaboration that unifies its global teams across India, USA, South Africa (a proud Level 2 B-BBEE Contributor), and UK.

We are seeking a highly analytical and business-focused Web Data Analyst to drive insights across our digital ecosystem. In this role, you will serve as the subject matter expert on customer behavior — analyzing how users discover, engage with, and purchase across our digital platforms. You will translate complex behavioral and transactional data into actionable insights that enhance user experience and optimize performance for a sophisticated customer base that includes designers, trade professionals, and end consumers.

This is a high-impact, cross-functional role partnering closely with E-Commerce, Marketing, Product, and Technology teams, operating within a modern digital stack including Bloomreach Discovery, Adobe Commerce, GA4, and enterprise BI platforms.

Job Title: Web Data Analyst (Mid–Senior Level)

Location- Houston, TX ( Hybrid )

Key Responsibilities:


Web Analytics & Performance Ownership
Own end-to-end web analytics governance, tracking accuracy, and KPI reporting.
Monitor and analyze key metrics including Conversion Rate (CVR), Average Order Value (AOV), revenue performance, funnel drop-offs, and session quality.
Identify trends, diagnose performance shifts, and deliver executive-ready insights with clear business narratives.
Partner with Product and E-Commerce teams to design, measure, and interpret A/B tests and personalization initiatives.
Quantify impact of digital enhancements and recommend data-driven optimizations.
Manage GA4 implementation, governance, and event tracking strategy.
Oversee Google Tag Manager (GTM) operations including:
Updating triggers and variables
Publishing across environments
Deploying and editing marketing pixels
Implementing analytics and marketing tracking solutions
Ensure tracking accuracy across marketing and product initiatives.
Data Engineering & Platform Support
Support GA4 to BigQuery exports including monitoring data transfers and basic SQL troubleshooting.
Maintain clean dataset organization and assist in resolving data discrepancies.
Configure and maintain analytics and marketing tools including:
Customer & Segment Analysis
Develop deep insights into distinct customer segments (trade professionals, designers, homeowners).
Analyze site search behavior, personalization performance, and campaign effectiveness.
Create and upload customer audience lists to paid media platforms.
Required Qualifications


4–6 years of experience in web or digital analytics, preferably in premium e-commerce or multi-channel retail.
Strong proficiency in SQL.
Hands-on expertise with GA4 and Google Tag Manager.
Experience with BI tools such as Looker or Power BI.
Working knowledge of A/B testing platforms.
Experience managing GA4–BigQuery integrations.
Strong understanding of tag governance and marketing pixel deployment



Flexible work from home options available.

Title: IT Support Specialist
Description:
GenBio AI develops multiscale foundation models to decode and simulate human biology. Our team is accelerating towards an ambitious future where scientists can unlock humanity's biggest challenges in drug discovery, healthcare, and fundamental research with AIDO (AI-Driven Digital Organism): a unified framework for predicting, simulating, and programming biology across all scales. The foundation of this vision begins today as we engineer the virtual cell to model and simulate the fundamental unit of life.

This vision has brought together a talent-dense group of product-minded researchers and engineers dedicated to bringing it to reality. Our team prides itself on our strong engineering culture and highly interdisciplinary and collaborative approach. We are based in Palo Alto, with satellite offices in Paris and Abu Dhabi.

As our team scales, we are looking for a highly skilled and motivated IT Specialist to join our Palo Alto office. Building large-scale foundation models and the AIDO framework requires a robust, secure, and high-performing technology infrastructure. In this role, you will manage and optimize our network, hardware, and systems, ensuring the reliability and security of the IT ecosystem that supports our cutting-edge AI research and development efforts globally.

Responsibilities


Network Management: Design, implement, and monitor secure network infrastructure across all office locations to support high-performance computing and resolve connectivity issues promptly.
System Administration: Manage Linux, Windows, and macOS server environments, overseeing the deployment of VMs and cloud services, alongside regular backups and disaster recovery planning.
Security Management: Enforce cybersecurity policies, conduct regular vulnerability assessments, and manage incident response to protect systems and sensitive data.
Technical Support: Manage the IT helpdesk to provide timely troubleshooting for hardware, software, and network issues while proactively establishing IT best practices.
Software Management: Configure and manage software applications and licenses, collaborating with development teams to deploy and optimize in-house solutions.
Hardware Management: Oversee the procurement, installation, and asset tracking of all IT equipment, continuously evaluating new technologies to enhance team productivity.
Project Management & Documentation: Lead cross-functional IT projects to align with business objectives, and maintain comprehensive documentation for system configurations, procedures, and policies.




Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field.
3+ years of experience in IT administration and support, preferably in a fast-paced startup environment.
Proficiency in managing Linux, Windows, and macOS systems, utilizing MDM solutions, particularly Jamf or JumpCloud.
Strong understanding of networking concepts (e.g., TCP/IP) and cybersecurity best practices, with hands-on experience deploying and managing security solutions like FortiNet VPN and firewalls.
Proven experience with cloud platforms, with a strong emphasis on Google Cloud Platform (GCP), alongside familiarity with AWS or Azure.
Extensive experience managing Google Workspace in a corporate environment, including writing automation scripts (e.g., integrating with HRIS/Payroll systems).
Hands-on experience with the administration of essential developer and productivity tools, including GitLab, Confluence, and Microsoft Office.
Proficient in configuring and using infrastructure monitoring and observability tools such as Grafana and ElasticSearch.
Excellent problem-solving, analytical, and interpersonal skills, with a proven ability to manage multiple tasks and IT projects simultaneously.




What We Offer


Competitive salary and benefits package.
Opportunity to work with a passionate and innovative team at the forefront of AI and biology.
Professional development and growth opportunities.




Join us as we embark on this journey to redefine the future of biology and medicine.

We are an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. GenBio AI participates in the U.S. Department of Homeland Security’s E-Verify program to confirm the employment eligibility of all newly hired employees. For more information on E-Verify, please visit www.e-verify.gov.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Data Entry Clerk
Description:
About The Company

Join our highly regarded multi-service law firm with over 60 years of excellence. Headquartered in Palm Beach Gardens with additional offices across Florida, our firm delivers sophisticated legal counsel to individuals, businesses, local governments, and institutional investors throughout the United States. Recognized with an AV rating from Martindale-Hubbell and ranked among the Top Law Firms in the country, we are known for our strong work ethic, efficiency, and results-driven approach to client service. Our commitment to maintaining a collaborative and innovative environment has positioned us as a leader in the legal industry, continuously striving to meet and exceed client expectations through dedicated expertise and personalized solutions.

About The Role

We are seeking a highly skilled Securities Law Attorney to join our dynamic team. This role offers an exciting opportunity to work on complex securities transactions, regulatory compliance, and corporate finance matters. The ideal candidate will advise clients on federal and state securities laws, prepare and file necessary disclosures, and manage a variety of transactions including private placements, public offerings, reverse mergers, and PIPE transactions. You will collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure regulatory adherence and facilitate seamless capital markets activities. This position requires a proactive professional capable of managing multiple projects independently while maintaining high standards of quality and compliance. The role provides a hybrid remote work environment, allowing for a flexible work-life balance while contributing to high-profile legal matters that impact clients across various industries.

Qualifications

The successful candidate must possess a Juris Doctor (J.D.) degree from an accredited law school and be licensed to practice law in Florida. A minimum of four years of experience focused on securities law is essential, with a proven track record advising on 1934 Act reporting and 1933 Act registered and exempt offerings. Experience handling SEC reporting and compliance, reverse mergers, venture capital, and private equity financings is highly desirable. Candidates should demonstrate the ability to independently manage filings, reports, and transaction execution, showcasing strong analytical, organizational, and communication skills. A comprehensive understanding of federal securities regulations, corporate finance, and transaction structuring is required to succeed in this role.

Responsibilities


Advise clients on federal and state securities law compliance, ensuring adherence to applicable regulations and statutes.
Prepare and file registration statements, including 10-Ks, 10-Qs, 8-Ks, proxy statements, and other disclosures required by regulatory authorities.
Handle complex transactions such as private placements, public offerings, reverse mergers, and PIPE transactions, guiding clients through each process.
Assist clients with uplisting and exchange compliance, including transitions from OTC to NASDAQ or NYSE platforms.
Counsel issuers, underwriters, and investors involved in capital markets transactions to optimize legal and regulatory outcomes.
Coordinate annual and special shareholder meetings and manage proxy-related matters to ensure compliance and smooth proceedings.
Respond to SEC comment letters and manage investigations conducted by SEC and FINRA, providing strategic legal guidance.
Oversee Regulation D, S, and A+ offerings, preparing related disclosure documentation and ensuring adherence to all regulatory requirements.
Collaborate with the Corporate department on mergers and acquisitions, as well as other corporate transactions, providing integrated legal support.


Benefits

Our firm offers a competitive salary structure, with base compensation up to $200,000+ depending on experience and qualifications. In addition, we provide a comprehensive bonus program that includes non-discretionary bonuses tied to billable hours and discretionary bonuses based on performance and origination efforts, with potential earnings up to $20,000 or more. Our benefits package includes medical, dental, vision, and life insurance, along with a matching 401(k) plan to support your financial future. We prioritize work-life balance by establishing clear bonus benchmarks and recognizing outstanding performance. The hybrid remote work environment offers flexibility, enabling you to balance professional growth with personal commitments, fostering a supportive and productive workplace culture.

Equal Opportunity

Jobot is an Equal Opportunity Employer committed to creating an inclusive and diverse work environment. We consider all qualified candidates without regard to race, color, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, religion, national origin, age (40 and over), disability, military status, genetic information, or any other protected characteristic under applicable federal, state, or local laws. We prohibit harassment or discrimination of any kind and are dedicated to ensuring equal employment opportunities for all applicants and employees. Our policies are designed to promote fairness, respect, and equal access to employment opportunities, fostering a workplace where everyone can thrive and contribute to our collective success.

Title: Business Analyst
Description:
About the Role

We are seeking a detail-oriented Business Analyst to evaluate business processes, identify improvement opportunities, and support data-driven decision-making. You will work closely with stakeholders to gather requirements and translate them into actionable insights.

Key Responsibilities
Analyze business needs and translate them into clear requirements
Conduct research, process mapping, and workflow documentation
Identify system or process gaps and propose improvement solutions
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to support project implementation
Prepare reports, dashboards, and presentations for management
Evaluate project outcomes and ensure alignment with business goals
Support data analysis and decision-making using various tools
Qualifications
Bachelor’s degree in Business, Economics, IT, or related field
2–4 years of experience as a Business Analyst or similar role
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Experience with requirement gathering and documentation
Familiarity with data analysis tools (Excel, SQL, Power BI, Tableau)
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills
Ability to work in a fast-paced environment and handle multiple priorities
Preferred Skills
Knowledge of Agile or Scrum methodologies
Experience with process improvement frameworks (Lean, Six Sigma)
Understanding of software development lifecycle (SDLC)
Strong presentation and reporting skills

Title: Manager, Data Analytics & Investigations
Description:
About Acadia Pharmaceuticals

Acadia is committed to turning scientific promise into meaningful innovation that makes the difference for underserved neurological and rare disease communities around the world. Our commercial portfolio includes the first and only FDA-approved treatments for Parkinson’s disease psychosis and Rett syndrome. We are developing the next wave of therapeutic advancements with a robust and diverse pipeline that includes mid- to late-stage programs in Alzheimer’s disease psychosis and Lewy body dementia psychosis, along with earlier-stage programs that address other underserved patient needs. At Acadia, we’re here to be their difference.

Please note that this position is based in Princeton, NJ. Acadia's hybrid model requires this role to work in our office three days per week on average.

Position Summary:

As the Manager, Data Analytics & Investigations, you will play a highly visible role in strengthening Acadia’s healthcare compliance program by leveraging data analytics, audits, and monitoring to proactively identify and mitigate risk. You will collaborate closely with cross‑functional partners across Compliance, Legal, Finance, and Commercial teams to ensure regulatory requirements, internal policies, and ethical standards are consistently upheld. This is an exciting opportunity for a compliance professional who enjoys turning complex data into actionable insights and making a meaningful impact on a growing organization’s integrity and culture.

Primary Responsibilities:

Data Analytics & Reporting


Lead and mature the organization’s healthcare compliance data analytics and monitoring program, ensuring scalability, sustainability, and alignment with regulatory expectations.
Design, develop, and maintain dashboards and analytical reporting using tools such as, Power BI and Excel to identify trends, anomalies, and compliance risk areas.
Analyze data from key compliance systems and sources (e.g., expense reporting, HCP interactions, samples activity, state filings, and field monitoring) to support proactive risk detection and remediation.
Partner with Compliance, Legal, Finance, and Commercial stakeholders to translate analytical insights into clear, actionable recommendations for leadership.
Leverage analytics to support investigations and root‑cause analysis, including pattern identification, scoping, and validation of investigative findings.
Continuously enhance data‑driven monitoring methodologies, helping to internalize analytics capabilities and strengthen overall compliance risk prioritization and training efforts.


Auditing & Monitoring


Develop and execute the annual healthcare compliance and privacy audit and monitoring plan (U.S. and Global).
Maintain and update monitoring documentation, workpapers, audit tools, remediation plans, and reporting templates.
Collaborate with stakeholders across Legal, Medical, Commercial, and Finance to gather relevant data, validate findings, and drive remediation.
Track, trend, and escalate monitoring results and areas of concern to leadership in a timely manner.
Utilize company data sources such as email, Teams, Concur expenses, medical inquiries, samples inventory, Veeva, Elevator, and other relevant platforms.
Apply relevant standards and regulations, including Code of Conduct, Compliance Roadmap, Employee Handbook, travel and expense SOP, internal policies, and applicable healthcare compliance and data privacy laws.


Investigations Support


Support intake, triage, and documentation of compliance and employee relations concerns received through monitoring activities, hotline, or other reporting channels.
Assist in fact-finding, document review, interview coordination, and root cause analysis during internal investigations.
Maintain investigation logs, outcome documentation, and remediation tracking consistent with company policies and legal requirements.
Ensure confidentiality, accuracy, and consistency in all investigative processes and outcomes.


Qualifications:


Bachelor’s degree in business, compliance, healthcare, law, or related field.
5+ years of experience in compliance, auditing, monitoring, or investigations within the pharmaceutical, biotech, or healthcare industry.
Strong understanding of healthcare compliance laws and regulations (e.g., Anti-Kickback Statute, False Claims Act, GDPR, HIPAA).
Experience working with monitoring and audit tools, systems, and data sources (Concur, Veeva, expense reporting, etc.).
Excellent analytical, organizational, and communication skills.
Ability to handle sensitive information with discretion and maintain strict confidentiality.
Experience in both U.S. and ex-U.S. regulatory environments.
Background in data analytics or forensics applied to compliance monitoring.
Prior experience supporting or leading investigations in a corporate setting.


Competencies:


Analytical Mindset: Ability to interpret data, identify trends, and assess risk.
Collaboration: Works effectively with cross-functional teams and external partners.
Ethical Judgment: Applies integrity and fairness in decision-making.
Communication: Clear and concise reporting to senior leadership.
Adaptability: Able to navigate changing regulatory landscapes and business priorities.


Physical Requirements:

This role involves regular standing, walking, sitting, and the use of hands for handling or operating equipment. The employee may also need to reach, climb, balance, stoop, kneel, crouch, and maintain visual, verbal, and auditory communication in a standard office environment and while working independently from remote locations. The employee must occasionally lift and/or move up to 20 pounds. This position requires the ability to travel independently overnight and/or work after hours as required by travel schedules or business needs.

In addition to a competitive base salary, this position is also eligible for discretionary bonus and equity awards based on factors such as individual and organizational performance. Actual amounts will vary depending on experience, performance, and location.

Salary Range

$108,000—$135,500 USD

What we offer US-based Employees:


Competitive base, bonus, new hire and ongoing equity packages
Medical, dental, and vision insurance
Employer-paid life, disability, business travel and EAP coverage
401(k) Plan with a fully vested company match 1:1 up to 5%
Employee Stock Purchase Plan with a 2-year purchase price lock-in
15+ vacation days
13 -15 paid holidays, including office closure between December 24th and January 1st
10 days of paid sick time
Paid parental leave benefit
Tuition assistance

Title: DDOT Business Systems Analyst Entry
Description:
Short Description:

Seeking a candidate to develop new Tableau dashboards and manage, maintain, and enhance existing ones based on supervisory needs. Role includes meetings with internal clients and stakeholders to gather requirements, provide updates, and ensure alignment.

Complete Description:


The Department of Transportation is seeking a candidate with extensive experience creating Tableau dashboards with Microsoft SQL database and other data sources. The ideal candidate will demonstrate excellent verbal and written communication skills, along with a strong sense of ownership and a project management mindset. This role requires the ability to independently create work plans, manage assigned projects and tasks, follow through on deliverables, and produce high-quality dashboards on time. All work must meet internal client expectations as well as supervisor standards.



Responsibilities:


Responsible for developing new Tableau dashboards and managing and improving existing Tableau dashboards per the supervisor's requests. Deliver high quality work on time.
Attend necessary meetings with internal clients and stakeholders on these dashboards to collect input and seek feedback. Present on dashboard drafts. Take meeting notes and make next-step action plans. Document tasks and progress per the supervisor's instructions.
Coordinate with IT, Database team, GIS team, data source vendors, and other necessary technical stakeholders to identify data sources, tables, and fields, for the dashboard creation.



Candidate Skills Matrix:

Following sections are to be filled by the candidate:

Skills | Required/Desired | No. of Years | How many years of experience candidate has?

Bachelor’s degree in IT or related field or equivalent experience | Required | 5 |

BA experience | Required | 2 |

Basic knowledge of business systems analysis, Tableau Dashboard Development | Required | 2 |

Familiar with data analysis, data visualization, dashboard maintenance, report development | Required | 2 |

Understanding requirements gathering, stakeholder communication, technical documentation, process improvement, and work flow analyisis | Required | 2 |

Strong organizational skills and attention to detail | Required | 2 |

Background check:


Extensive criminal history background check will be required. We cannot submit candidates with recent histories (go back seven years) of extensive driving, drug, robbery or any other illegal activity. Any criminal activity on the background check will eliminate the candidate from consideration. If selected, please make certain that you inform all candidates that they will have to complete this criminal background check prior to starting. NATIONAL background checks are required; Federal background checks are NOT compliant under this contract. A national background check is a national criminal background check that pulls criminal records from State and County Courts in almost every US State.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
💚Softtek es un socio global de ingeniería de software que impulsa el progreso de las compañías. Desde hace más de 40 años, hemos ayudado a nuestros clientes a desarrollar, implementar y gestionar tecnología que mejora la vida de las personas.




Con presencia en más de 20 países y más de 16,000 softtekians en todo el mundo, en Softtek estamos en constante crecimiento. En España, te ofrecemos la oportunidad de unirte a nuestra comunidad en ubicaciones como Albacete, Asturias, Ávila, Barcelona, Córdoba, Coruña, Madrid, Narón, Segovia o Zaragoza, donde podrás desarrollar tu carrera dentro de un modelo híbrido y flexible.




En 2025, hemos sido reconocidos en la Lista Forbes como una de las 100 mejores empresas para trabajar en España, destacando por nuestra flexibilidad y personalización en la experiencia del empleado. Creemos en el poder de las habilidades diversas y la la iniciativa personal para lograr cosas asombrosas, y valoramos el talento único de cada persona para contribuir al crecimiento y diversidad de nuestro equipo. ¿Nos acompañas en esta aventura? #FutureTogether




👀 ¿Te interesa trabajar en el diseño de modelos de reporting y seguimiento de proyectos, con impacto directo en la toma de decisiones de Dirección?




Buscamos perfiles Data Analyst con fuerte dominio en Power BI y automatización, para trabajar en un entorno de gestión de portfolio y reporting ejecutivo.




🔍 ¿Qué esperamos de ti?




Experiencia en Power BI (nivel avanzado) 4-6 años de experiencia.
Experiencia en Power Automate (automatización de flujos).
Experiencia con Jira
Experiencia en reporting y análisis de datos.
Nivel avanzado en Excel y paquete Office.
Experiencia creando presentaciones ejecutivas (PowerPoint).




💻 ¿Cómo será tu día a día?




Diseñarás y mantendrás el modelo de reporting y seguimiento de proyectos, utilizando Jira como fuente principal de datos y herramientas como Power BI y Power Automate para construir dashboards y automatizaciones.
Trabajarás en la creación de informes multinivel (negocio, portfolio, delivery y dirección), asegurando la calidad, coherencia y trazabilidad de la información. Además, apoyarás el reporting mensual a Dirección, analizando evolución de proyectos, indicadores, incidencias y datos financieros cuando aplique.
Colaborarás estrechamente con equipos de portfolio y stakeholders, proponiendo mejoras continuas y evolucionando el modelo de reporting




✅Conoce nuestra propuesta de valor

📝Contrato indefinido.

💻Modelo híbrido: Súmate a nuestro modelo Softtek 56, que se adapta a las circunstancias de cada Softtekian y en donde podrás conectar y desarrollarte con nuestros equipos en las diferentes sedes 56 horas al mes y disfruta de teletrabajo el resto del tiempo.

👪 Conciliación: Horario flexible y el programa BetterWork que apoya el equilibrio entre tu vida personal y profesional.

✈️24 días de vacaciones.

🩺 Seguro médico.

💳Retribución flexible: tarjeta transporte, tarjeta restaurante, cheque guardería o seguro médico familiares.

🎂 Tarde de cumpleaños libre (también por el cumple de tus hijos).

🎁 Club del empleado: Beneficios y promociones exclusivas por ser Softtekian.

🎓Planes de formación: idiomas, tecnologías y habilidades interpersonales.

🌍 Oportunidad de movilidad interna: en nuestras sedes internacionales y dentro de España.




🔝 Valoraremos especialmente la candidatura de personas que posean habilidades y capacidades únicas y diversas, reconociendo la importancia de facilitar su integración plena en el mercado laboral. Cada individuo, con sus capacidades singulares, aporta una riqueza invaluable a nuestra comunidad, y nuestro compromiso es asegurar que todos tengan la oportunidad de demostrar su valía y contribuir al crecimiento y la diversidad de nuestro equipo.

Title: ASSISTANT DATA ANALYST - H/F
Description:
Description

Rejoindre le GALEC à Toulouse, c'est intégrer une équipe agile au coeur de la stratégie d'E.Leclerc DRIVE (plus de 950 sites). Votre rôle ? Transformer la donnée en boussole pour tous les métiers (Marketing, Logistique, Offre, Communication).

Les missions : Un quotidien rythmé et évolutif

Le Poste S'articule Autour De Trois Piliers Complémentaires, Vous Offrant Une Vision Complète Du Cycle De La Donnée


Pilotage & Business Intelligence
Assurer le suivi de l'activité nationale E.Leclerc Drive
Déployer et optimiser des tableaux de bord via Looker Studio et Microstrategy.
Garantir la fiabilité des indicateurs clés pour les directions stratégiques.
Études & Support Métiers
Répondre aux sollicitations "ad hoc" des différents pôles
Traduire des besoins business complexes en requêtes de données et en analyses synthétiques.
Modélisation & Projets Exploratoires :En fonction de l'actualité du Drive et de vos affinités, vous mènerez des projets statistiques avancés, par exemple :
Prévisions : Modélisation du chiffre d'affaires ou des volumes.
Segmentation & Scoring : Analyse fine des comportements clients.
Algorithmique : Algo de recommandation ou d'optimisation.


Votre stack technique


Extraction et manipulation : Maîtrise de SAS et/ou SQL.
Analyses avancées : Python.
Visualisation : Looker Studio, Microstrategy.

HTML:
Description

Rejoindre le GALEC à Toulouse, c'est intégrer une équipe agile au coeur de la stratégie d'E.Leclerc DRIVE (plus de 950 sites). Votre rôle ? Transformer la donnée en boussole pour tous les métiers (Marketing, Logistique, Offre, Communication).

Les missions : Un quotidien rythmé et évolutif

Le Poste S'articule Autour De Trois Piliers Complémentaires, Vous Offrant Une Vision Complète Du Cycle De La Donnée

  • Pilotage & Business Intelligence
  • Assurer le suivi de l'activité nationale E.Leclerc Drive
  • Déployer et optimiser des tableaux de bord via Looker Studio et Microstrategy.
  • Garantir la fiabilité des indicateurs clés pour les directions stratégiques.
  • Études & Support Métiers
  • Répondre aux sollicitations "ad hoc" des différents pôles
  • Traduire des besoins business complexes en requêtes de données et en analyses synthétiques.
  • Modélisation & Projets Exploratoires :En fonction de l'actualité du Drive et de vos affinités, vous mènerez des projets statistiques avancés, par exemple :
  • Prévisions : Modélisation du chiffre d'affaires ou des volumes.
  • Segmentation & Scoring : Analyse fine des comportements clients.
  • Algorithmique : Algo de recommandation ou d'optimisation.

Votre stack technique

  • Extraction et manipulation : Maîtrise de SAS et/ou SQL.
  • Analyses avancées : Python.
  • Visualisation : Looker Studio, Microstrategy.

Title: Data analyst - Power BI
Description:
Location: Spain

Role Type: Permanent

Key Skills: Power BI, DAX, SQL, Python, Azure

Working model: REMOTE - SPAIN

Fluent English required

Must be based in Spain




JOB RESPONSIBILITIES / ROLE

+ 3 years of experience in designing, developing, and maintaining Power BI dashboards and reports for business‑critical use cases.
Translate business requirements into data models, KPIs, and actionable visual insights.
Collaborate closely with domain teams to ensure consistent and trusted reporting across the organisation.
Perform data analysis and data exploration using Python and/or SQL.
Work with Data Engineers to ensure data quality, availability, and performance of analytical datasets.
Contribute to data validation and QA processes, including emerging agent‑based approaches.
Support stakeholders in understanding data and promoting data‑driven decision making.
Ensure compliance with data governance standards and domain‑oriented data principles.




MANDATORY SKILLS & EXPERIENCE

Proven experience as a Data Analyst in data‑driven environments (banking or financial services is a strong plus).
Strong expertise in Power BI, including data modelling, DAX, and performance optimisation.
Solid knowledge of SQL and data querying techniques.
Experience using Python for data analysis (e.g. Pandas).
Good understanding of data warehousing concepts and analytical data models.
Experience working in Azure data environments (such as Azure Data Lake, Synapse, Databricks).
Ability to work with large‑scale datasets and distributed environments (Spark is a plus).
Strong analytical mindset with the ability to translate data into clear, actionable insights.
Excellent communication skills and ability to collaborate with technical and non‑technical stakeholders.
