Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Decskill was founded in 2014 as an IT Consulting Company and their main mission is to delivery value through the knowledge. We enable companies to meet the chalenges of digital world by providing our clients with business models that ensure technological capacity, flexibility and agility. We are more than 500 consultants with offices in Lisbon, Porto and Madrid.




DECSKILL operates in 3 main areas:




DECSKILL TALENT, through which we provide our clients with an extension to their IT teams;




DECSKILL BOOST, through which we provide our client with software development models to increase capacity and optimize Time-to-Market, where we create and manage teams that deliver according to their needs, at the desired speed;




DECSKILL CONNECT through which we provide our client with consulting services, as well as the implementation and management of information technology infrastructures.




Our practice results in the creation of value for our customers, either by delivering qualified and value-added services, or through highly qualified and motivated professionals, as well as technology solutions that allow us to operate and transform the business of our customers.




We are looking for a Data Analyst:




RESPONSABILITIES:

As a Data Analyst, you will play a vital role in our team.
Working directly with one or more of the bank's business areas, you will provide valuable insights to drive business growth, understading their needs, proposing and implementing data-driven solutions (analysis, visualization and automation). You will exercise a lot of problem identification and problem solving skills.
You will have the opportunity to take ownership of business essential analytical projects that involve retrieving, preparing and analysing complex datasets using data/statistical models and best practices in data visualization for the reporting creation, with the goal to produce insights and automated tools that will guide strategic solutions
You will move between technical and non-technical areas and should be able to communicate well with different types of professionals.
It is very important to be able to clearly and concisely understand stekeholders’ needs and translate these to our technical team, as well as present the results of complex analysis to our stakeholders.




TECHNICAL SKILLS:

Academic background in Engineering, Economics, Computing, Mathematics, Statistics or related fields
Professional experience in any data visualization tool such as Tableau or Power BI
Professional experience in data analysis with programming, preferably Python (Pandas)
Professional experience in any data visualization tool such as Tableau or Power BI
Professional experience in data analysis with programming, preferably Python (Pandas)
Ability to analysze, synthetize and simplify.
Knowledge of descriptive statistics and hypothesis testing is a plus.
Knowledge in Big Data technology such as Pyspark, Presto and Airflow is a plus
Knowledge of Power Query and DAX is a plus




IDIOMAS:

Inglês - Avançado- (Obrigatório)
Francês - Noções - PLUS




If you’re interested in this job, please send your CV to carolina.mustra@decskill.com with reference “CM/Data Analyst”




Thank You! 😊




Decskill is committed to equality and non-discrimination with all our talents. We recruit and promote talent, based on diversity and inclusion, regardless of age, gender, ethnicity, race, nationality or any other form of discrimination incompatible with the dignity of the human being

Title: Business & Data Analyst
Description:
¡En Ayesa Digital crecemos Contigo!




Cada profesional de nuestra empresa es importante para nosotros, nos ayudan a crecer de manera diversa y gracias a ellos somos 11.000 personas trabajando con el mismo objetivo en 10 países.




Si eres una persona entusiasta y buscas un nuevo reto profesional, ¡Este es tu sitio!




¡Anímate a dar el salto! ¡Te estamos esperando! Buscamos un/a Business & Data Analyst con fuerte orientación a ecommerce, capaz de transformar datos en insights accionables que impulsen el crecimiento del negocio. La persona seleccionada trabajará de forma transversal con equipos de Data, Comercial y Desarrollo, asegurando una visión integral del rendimiento del ecommerce.




¿Qué vas a hacer?




Analizar el rendimiento del ecommerce y detectar oportunidades de mejora.
Extraer, transformar y analizar datos para generar insights estratégicos.
Trabajar estrechamente con los equipos de Data, Comercial y Desarrollo para alinear objetivos y acciones.
Construir dashboards e informes en Power BI y otras herramientas de visualización.
Analizar el comportamiento de usuarios mediante Google Analytics.
Realizar consultas y análisis avanzados en SQL.
Presentar resultados y recomendaciones a clientes o stakeholders internos.
Traducir necesidades de negocio en requerimientos analíticos claros.




Requisitos:




Experiencia sólida en análisis de negocio y análisis de datos en ecommerce.
Dominio de: Google Analytics, SQL, Power BI
Entendimiento profundo del funcionamiento de un ecommerce (funnels, conversión, ticket medio, cohortes, etc.).
Capacidad analítica y pensamiento estratégico.
Excelente comunicación y capacidad para interactuar con cliente y equipos multidisciplinares.
Perfil proactivo, orientado a resultados y con visión de negocio.




Te ofrecemos un camino lleno de aventuras:

· ¡Llega hasta donde tú quieras! ¡Crece con nuestros programas de desarrollo! Te

ayudaremos a marcar tu itinerario de futuro anualmente participando en proyectos

vanguardistas con los mejores profesionales del sector.

· Porque lo importante sois tú y los tuyos : contamos con políticas que facilitan la

conciliación entre la vida profesional y personal: Flexibilidad horaria, Smart Job y jornada

intensiva en verano.

· Tú decides cómo usar tu dinero: disfruta de nuestra retribución flexible, contamos con:

Seguro médico, Ticket Restaurant, Guardería y Transporte.

· Plan Bienestar descuentos en viajes, tecnología, formación, deporte, belleza y ¡muchos

más!

· Diviértete participando en los diferentes concursos y actividades que organizamos: ¿te

gusta el deporte, escribir, la fotografía? Todo tiene cabida.

· ¡Actualízate! apostamos por un aprendizaje continuo, contamos con formación

especializada, certificaciones e idiomas




· ¡Deja tu huella! Impacta con nuestros proyectos de voluntariado social.




¡Únete a la multinacional española tecnológica referente en el mundo!

Atendiendo a la Ley Orgánica 3/2007, de 22 de marzo, la empresa se ha marcado como objetivo promover la defensa y aplicación efectiva del principio de igualdad entre hombres y mujeres, evitando cualquier tipo de discriminación laboral por razón de sexo, garantizando así las mismas oportunidades de ingreso. Asimismo, fomentamos la diversidad, rechazando cualquier tipo de discriminación por razones de raza, sexo, diversidad funcional, religión, orientación sexual, identidad sexual, o cualquier otra condición personal o social, apostando por construir un entorno inclusivo y enriquecedor.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Qui sommes-nous ?




Depuis 2016, nous accompagnons nos clients sur des problématiques de Product Management, Data et Design Thinking. Beelix contribue à façonner le monde de demain en participant aux grandes avancées des secteurs suivants :




🚗Automobile

⚡Energie

📡Médias & Télécoms

👗Luxe & Retail

💶 Banque, Finance & Assurance

✈️Défense




Aujourd’hui, Beelix compte plus de 200 collaborateurs motivés et dynamiques. Labélisée Great Place To work en 2023, Beelix est aussi une entreprise engagée où il fait bon vivre.




Dans le cadre de notre développement, nous recherchons un Data Analyst IT (F/H) à Montpellier pour l'un de nos clients.




Quelles missions au quotidien ?




Rattaché(e) à l’équipe Data, vous intervenez sur l’analyse et la valorisation des données afin d’accompagner les équipes métiers dans leurs décisions stratégiques.




Dans ce cadre, vous serez amené(e) à :

Extraire et manipuler des données volumineuses via SQL
Développer, optimiser et maintenir des requêtes SQL complexes
Intervenir sur des bases de données telles que Teradata (ou PostgreSQL / MySQL)
Concevoir, produire et automatiser des reportings
Créer des tableaux de bord interactifs avec MicroStrategy (ou Power BI / Tableau)
Analyser les données afin d’identifier des tendances et insights
Collaborer étroitement avec les équipes métiers pour comprendre et formaliser les besoins




A propos de vous ?




Formation Bac +3 à Bac +5 en informatique, data ou statistiques
Expérience significative en tant que Data Analyst
Maîtrise avancée de SQL
Bonne connaissance d’au moins un SGBD : Teradata, PostgreSQL ou MySQL
Expérience sur un outil de data visualisation : MicroStrategy, Power BI ou Tableau
Expérience dans les secteurs bancaires ou financiers
Esprit analytique, rigueur et autonomie
Bon relationnel et capacité à vulgariser les données
Anglais lu et parlé obligatoire.




Beelix c’est aussi une structure qui propose :




Un suivi et un accompagnement au quotidien ( points réguliers, déjeuners…)

Un organisme de formation certifié Qualiopi, un abonnement linkedin learning pour chaque salarié et des partenariats avec des spécialistes pour d’autres expertises

De nombreux événements : Afterworks, Communautés métiers, Happy talks…

une Expérience personnalisée basée sur vos besoins grâce au Prédictive Index

Notre package « unBeelievable » : 100% du titre de transport, CSE, Prime de participation...




Le processus de recrutement ?




Échange téléphonique (15 min)

Entretien 1 RH pour apprendre à vous connaître

Entretien 2 avec votre futur N+1 pour appréhender la relation managériale

Entretien 3 avec un Responsable commercial pour avoir la vision stratégique

  • Knowledge of descriptive statistics and hypothesis testing is a plus.
  • Knowledge in Big Data technology such as Pyspark, Presto and Airflow is a plus
  • Knowledge of Power Query and DAX is a plus


IDIOMAS:

  • Inglês - Avançado- (Obrigatório)
  • Francês - Noções - PLUS


If you’re interested in this job, please send your CV to carolina.mustra@decskill.com with reference “CM/Data Analyst”


Thank You! 😊


Decskill is committed to equality and non-discrimination with all our talents. We recruit and promote talent, based on diversity and inclusion, regardless of age, gender, ethnicity, race, nationality or any other form of discrimination incompatible with the dignity of the human being

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Estamos buscando un/a Analista de Datos – Área de Compras (Alicante)




¿Te apasionan los datos y su impacto en la toma de decisiones? Buscamos incorporar una persona a nuestras oficinas centrales en Alicante para dar soporte estratégico al área de Compras a través del análisis y reporting de datos.




🎯 Misión del puesto

Dar apoyo al área de Compras mediante el análisis de datos, trabajando estrechamente con el equipo de Compras, el Director de Procurement y los Category Managers, con el objetivo de facilitar el seguimiento del negocio y la toma de decisiones.




🧩 ¿Qué harás en tu día a día?

Recopilar y extraer datos de distintas fuentes.
Preparar y depurar grandes volúmenes de datos para asegurar su calidad.
Analizar y visualizar datos mediante informes, gráficos y presentaciones.
Apoyar en los procesos de presupuestación y planificación anual de KPIs de Compras.
Elaborar informes de seguimiento (semanales, mensuales y anuales).
Colaborar con equipos multifuncionales (IT, Finanzas, Aprovisionamiento…).
Participar activamente en la implementación de nuevas herramientas de reporting.
Colaborar en la implantación de un nuevo ERP a nivel grupo.




✅ Requisitos

Titulación universitaria.
Experiencia de 1–2 años en puestos similares.
Dominio avanzado de Excel, Power BI y Power Query.
Se valorará conocimiento de SAP (no imprescindible).
Nivel de inglés C1.




🧠 Competencias clave

Alta capacidad analítica y pensamiento crítico.
Capacidad de síntesis y orientación a resultados.
Buenas habilidades de comunicación.
Trabajo en equipo y colaboración transversal.




🎁 ¿Qué ofrecemos?

Contrato indefinido y jornada completa.
Salario competitivo y beneficios adicionales.
Desarrollo profesional y formación continua.
Entorno de trabajo dinámico y colaborativo.

📩 ¿Te interesa? ¡Nos encantará conocerte!

Title: Business Data Analyst – BI & Advanced Analytics
Description:
CEVA Logistics offre des solutions globales de chaîne d'approvisionnement pour connecter les personnes, les produits et les fournisseurs du monde entier. Présents dans 170 pays et avec plus de 110 000 employés répartis sur 1 500 sites, nous sommes en bonne voie pour atteindre notre objectif : être l'un des cinq principaux prestataires de services logistiques (3PL) dans le monde.

Nous sommes convaincus que nos employés sont la clé de notre succès. Nous voulons engager et autonomiser notre équipe mondiale et diversifiée pour co-créer de la valeur avec nos clients grâce à nos solutions en logistique contractuelle et en transport aérien, maritime, terrestre et de véhicules finis. C'est pourquoi CEVA Logistics offre un environnement de travail dynamique et exceptionnel qui favorise la croissance personnelle, l'innovation et l'amélioration continue.

OSEZ GRANDIR ! Rejoignez CEVA Logistics, et vous ferez partie d'une équipe qui valorise l'imagination, encourage l'audace et l'exemplarité, et s'engage pour l'excellence dans tout ce que nous faisons. Rejoignez-nous dans notre mission de façonner l'avenir de la logistique mondiale alors que nous devenons un leader mondial de l'industrie de la logistique. Alors que nous continuons de croître à un rythme rapide, allez-vous "Oser grandir" avec nous ?







Votre rôle :

Au sein de l’équipe Operational Performance & Efficiency de CEVA Logistics, vous jouez un rôle clé dans l’analyse et l’exploitation des données afin de piloter la performance des programmes logistiques à l’échelle internationale.

Vous intervenez à la frontière entre les équipes métiers, les équipes data et les équipes opérationnelles pour transformer des données complexes en analyses claires, exploitables et orientées décision.

Votre rôle combine analyse business, BI et exploitation avancée de la donnée pour accompagner la performance des activités Contract Logistics.




Vos missions :




Analyse & pilotage de la donnée

Analyse de la performance & Advanced Analytics

Analyser des volumes de données pour identifier tendances, anomalies et leviers de performance

Construire des analyses approfondies pour éclairer les décisions business

Suivre et challenger les KPI des programmes internationaux




BI & Reporting avancé

Concevoir, développer et améliorer des dashboards BI (Power BI / Tableau)

Automatiser les reportings et fiabiliser les indicateurs de performance

Structurer des analyses récurrentes pour les équipes métiers et Solution Design




Traduction business / data

Comprendre les besoins métiers et les transformer en analyses data exploitables

Identifier les problématiques opérationnelles à travers la donnée

Apporter des recommandations basées sur les analyses réalisées




Qualité & structuration de la donnée

Garantir la cohérence et la fiabilité des données utilisées

Travailler sur la structuration et l’amélioration des datasets

Collaborer avec les équipes data pour assurer la qualité des flux




Nous recherchons :

Formation en Data, BI, Statistiques, Économie, Informatique ou équivalent
Première expérience ou jeune profil avec forte exposition à l’analyse de données
Très bonne maîtrise de SQL indispensable
Très bonne maîtrise d’Excel et outils BI (Power BI / Tableau)
Capacité à manipuler, structurer et analyser des données complexes
Bonne compréhension des enjeux business et opérationnels
Capacité à aller au-delà du reporting pour produire de l’analyse utile
Anglais indispensable (environnement international)
Bon relationnel et capacité à travailler avec des équipes métiers




En tant qu'organisation mondiale, et en appartenant au groupe CMA CGM, la diversité est essentielle à la réussite de notre entreprise; ce n'est que lorsque nous pouvons refléter les cultures, les langues, les comportements et les connaissances locales de nos clients que nous pouvons réussir. En employant des personnes ayant des expériences et des capacités différentes, nous élargissons nos connaissances et augmentons notre créativité et notre innovation.

Veuillez noter : les processus de recrutement officiels de CEVA Logistics comprennent la communication avec les candidats par l'intermédiaire de réseaux professionnels reconnus, tels que LinkedIn ou via une adresse électronique officielle de l'entreprise : firstname.lastname@cevalogistics.com. Nous vous recommandons de ne pas répondre à des propositions et/ou offres commerciales non sollicitées émanant de personnes que vous ne connaissez pas.

Location: Brussels, Belgium

Type: Contract

Start Date: ASAP/June

Duration: 6 Months + Extensions

Languages: English/Dutch

Strong experience working as a Data Analyst
Strong SQL skills with the ability to analyse, manipulate, and validate large datasets.
Hands on experience with Power BI including dashboard creation, reporting, data modelling, and KPI visualisation.

Excellent analytical, communication, and problem solving skills.
Strong attention to detail and ability to work in fast-paced environments.

Title: Data analyst
Description:
Únete a Amaris Consulting y participa en iniciativas data-driven que impactan a millones de usuarias de moda en todo el mundo. Formarás parte de un grupo internacional, trabajando implant (FTE) en el equipo de Ecommerce en un entorno que valora la innovación, la autonomía y el talento, utilizando un stack de datos real y de última generación.




🎯 Tu Misión & Impacto




Como Data analyst te incorporarás al equipo de Ecommerce para transformar datos en insights accionables, modelos analíticos y recomendaciones estratégicas que impulsen la captación, conversión, venta y retención de clientas, así como la reducción de devoluciones.




Trabajarás mano a mano con stakeholders de negocio digital, CRM, paid media y tecnología, asegurando que las decisiones clave se basen en datos y contribuyendo directamente al crecimiento del canal online.




🛠 Responsabilidades




Analizar datos de clientes y comportamiento digital para mejorar:Captación, conversión, venta, fidelización y devoluciones.
Diseñar, crear y mantener dashboards y reporting en Power BI para los equipos de Ecommerce y negocio.
Definir y hacer seguimiento de KPIs clave de Ecommerce (tráfico, conversión, AOV, churn, LTV, etc.) para apoyar la toma de decisiones estratégicas.
Integrar y cruzar datos digitales (web/app) con:CRM, paid media y datos transaccionales.
Desarrollar modelos analíticos y predictivos orientados a negocio:Segmentación de clientes, scoring, propensión a compra, forecasting de demanda, etc.
Velar por la calidad del dato, proponiendo mejoras en procesos y fuentes de información.
Preparar y comunicar insights y resultados de manera clara a los distintos stakeholders (Ecommerce, Marketing, Producto, Tech).
Colaborar en iniciativas de experimentación digital (A/B testing) para optimizar funnels, campañas y experiencias de usuario.




✅ Requisitos




Experiencia previa en entornos Ecommerce (retail, moda, marketplaces o similares).
Dominio avanzado de SQL.
Experiencia trabajando con BigQuery y Snowflake.
Dominio avanzado de Power BI (modelado de datos, DAX, visualizaciones).
Nivel avanzado de Excel.
Nivel alto de inglés (entorno internacional, documentación y reuniones).




🌟 Se valorará positivamente




Experiencia en modelos predictivos:Propensión a compra, churn, LTV, forecasting de demanda, recomendación de productos, etc.
Conocimiento de técnicas de machine learning (supervisado y no supervisado).
Experiencia con Python (pandas, numpy, scikit-learn).
Uso de notebooks (Databricks Notebooks / Jupyter).
Experiencia en A/B testing y experimentación digital.
Uso de herramientas como GrowthBook y Optimizely.
Experiencia trabajando con Azure DevOps y flujos de trabajo colaborativos (versionado, CI/CD de modelos o pipelines).




🧩 Tech Stack




Trabajarás con un stack moderno y orientado a grandes volúmenes de datos:

Data & Cloud: BigQuery, Snowflake, Databricks
Lenguajes: SQL, Python
Reporting & BI: Power BI, Excel
Experimentación: GrowthBook, Optimizely
Colaboración & DevOps: Azure DevOps




🏙 Ubicación & Modelo de trabajo




Posición basada en Cerdanyola del Valles, Barcelona, España.
Se dispone de autobus de empresa.
Presencialidad 2 días a la semana.
Entorno multidisciplinar con fuerte foco en negocio digital, datos y tecnología.




🚀 Por qué unirte a Amaris Consulting




Crecimiento profesional: el 70% de nuestros líderes senior comenzaron en posiciones junior.
Formación continua: acceso a academias internas, formaciones online, webinars y certificaciones patrocinadas por la compañía.
Flexibilidad & beneficios: modelo de trabajo híbrido y paquete competitivo de beneficios sociales (a detallar durante el proceso).
Bienestar: nuestro programa WeCare te acompaña en situaciones críticas.
Desarrollo personal: presupuesto anual de formación para potenciar tus habilidades y acelerar tu carrera.




📅 Fecha de incorporación: ASAP

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
A Job? Or a Lifetime Experience? Start Yours Here!




A nossa missão é ser uma parte significativa da carreira das nossas pessoas. À medida que crescemos, cresce também a determinação em oferecer a melhor experiência aos nossos colaboradores e clientes e é exatamente isso que nos move. Somos uma empresa portuguesa de consultoria tecnológica com escritórios em Lisboa, Porto e Óbidos e representações no Brasil e Tunísia. Contamos com mais de 13 anos de expertise no mercado, e hoje somos um universo de cerca de 400 pessoas a trabalhar em contexto presencial, remoto ou híbrido em projetos em mais de 20 países.




Acreditamos que grandes pessoas fazem empresas de sucesso e por isso mesmo defendemos a valorização, reconhecimento e crescimento dos nossos profissionais.




Não queremos ser mais uma linha do teu CV, queremos que connosco vivas uma Lifetime Experience. Apostamos em formações e certificações que confirmam a tua real progressão, na promoção de um saudável work-life balance e na oferta de benefícios com impacto na tua vida pessoal e carreira.




O que procuramos

Formação superior em Computer Science, Data Science ou área equivalente
Sólidos conhecimentos em SQL
Experiência em Data Visualization, Business Intelligence e processos de ETL
Domínio de ferramentas de visualização como Power BI, Tableau, Qlik ou SAP Analytics Cloud
Fluência na língua inglesa (mínimo B2)




Ser Affinity

#Affinidade – Criares relações de proximidade e empatia com colegas, clientes e candidatos (e não apenas com tecnologias, linguagens e plataformas). Espírito de equipa e boa disposição;
#Ambição – Quereres fazer mais e melhor. Vontade de exceder expectativas e evoluir pessoal e profissionalmente;
#Ação – Energia para fazer acontecer. Mostrares proatividade e capacidade de iniciativa;
#Aprendizagem – Vontade de evoluir individual e coletivamente e tornares-te um expert no mercado tecnológico.




A tua #LifetimeExperience

Participar em projetos nacionais e internacionais numa empresa baseada em relacionamentos pessoais, na simplicidade e na eficiência, com uma ação disruptiva no mercado tecnológico;
Aceder a um plano de carreira e formação personalizado e diretamente proporcional ao teu desempenho e interesse;
Fazer parte de um ambiente acolhedor, de confiança, respeito e informalidade;
Integrar as nossas Affinity Communities (de desporto, tecnologia, hobbies, etc.) e participar ativamente em projetos de responsabilidade social e ambiental;
Tirar partido de um conjunto de benefícios, parcerias, descontos eventos e dinâmicas internas.




Para além da tua satisfação profissional queremos proporcionar-te oportunidades de lazer e convívio memoráveis e dignos de uma experiência Affinity.




Join the Experience!




Envia-nos a tua candidatura e aproveita para nos conhecer e ir acompanhando em:




https://www.instagram.com/affinityportugal/

https://www.linkedin.com/company/2701281/admin/

https://twitter.com/WithAffinity

Somos uma empresa portuguesa de consultoria tecnológica com escritórios em Lisboa, Porto e Óbidos e representações no Brasil e Tunísia. Contamos com mais de 13 anos de expertise no mercado, e hoje somos um universo de cerca de 400 pessoas a trabalhar em contexto presencial, remoto ou híbrido em projetos em mais de 20 países.


Acreditamos que grandes pessoas fazem empresas de sucesso e por isso mesmo defendemos a valorização, reconhecimento e crescimento dos nossos profissionais.


Não queremos ser mais uma linha do teu CV, queremos que connosco vivas uma Lifetime Experience. Apostamos em formações e certificações que confirmam a tua real progressão, na promoção de um saudável work-life balance e na oferta de benefícios com impacto na tua vida pessoal e carreira.


O que procuramos

  • Formação superior em Computer Science, Data Science ou área equivalente
  • Sólidos conhecimentos em SQL
  • Experiência em Data Visualization, Business Intelligence e processos de ETL
  • Domínio de ferramentas de visualização como Power BI, Tableau, Qlik ou SAP Analytics Cloud
  • Fluência na língua inglesa (mínimo B2)


Ser Affinity

  • #Affinidade – Criares relações de proximidade e empatia com colegas, clientes e candidatos (e não apenas com tecnologias, linguagens e plataformas). Espírito de equipa e boa disposição;
  • #Ambição – Quereres fazer mais e melhor. Vontade de exceder expectativas e evoluir pessoal e profissionalmente;
  • #Ação – Energia para fazer acontecer. Mostrares proatividade e capacidade de iniciativa;
  • #Aprendizagem – Vontade de evoluir individual e coletivamente e tornares-te um expert no mercado tecnológico.


A tua #LifetimeExperience

  • Participar em projetos nacionais e internacionais numa empresa baseada em relacionamentos pessoais, na simplicidade e na eficiência, com uma ação disruptiva no mercado tecnológico;
  • Aceder a um plano de carreira e formação personalizado e diretamente proporcional ao teu desempenho e interesse;
  • Fazer parte de um ambiente acolhedor, de confiança, respeito e informalidade;
  • Integrar as nossas Affinity Communities (de desporto, tecnologia, hobbies, etc.) e participar ativamente em projetos de responsabilidade social e ambiental;
  • Tirar partido de um conjunto de benefícios, parcerias, descontos eventos e dinâmicas internas.


Para além da tua satisfação profissional queremos proporcionar-te oportunidades de lazer e convívio memoráveis e dignos de uma experiência Affinity.


Join the Experience!


Envia-nos a tua candidatura e aproveita para nos conhecer e ir acompanhando em:


https://www.instagram.com/affinityportugal/

https://www.linkedin.com/company/2701281/admin/

https://twitter.com/WithAffinity

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst – Contract | Germany | Power BI & Python Analytics




Digisourced Ltd is supporting a client engagement and is seeking an experienced Data Analyst to support the development of advanced analytics and reporting solutions within a large-scale operational environment.




📅 Start: June 2026

📆 Duration: Until December 2026

💼 Engagement: Contract / Freelance or Employment

🌍 Location: 90% Remote / up to 10% onsite in Frankfurt

🏠 Candidate Location: Based in Germany

🗣 Language: German




Role Overview

This role focuses on the development of data analytics, reporting, and BI solutions using Python, SQL, and Power BI within a cloud-based data environment. You will support the creation of data-driven insights and interactive reporting solutions in a complex operational landscape.




Key Responsibilities

Develop and maintain complex data analyses using Python and SQL
Design and optimise interactive Power BI dashboards and reports
Build and maintain Power BI Dataflows for centralised data preparation
Connect and analyse data from cloud-based data platforms (AWS/Amazon S3)
Create and optimise semantic data models and KPIs using DAX
Ensure data quality, validation, and monitoring
Support implementation of security and governance concepts within reporting environments




Required Experience

Strong experience in Python-based data analysis
Extensive experience with Power BI, DAX, and Dataflows
Experience with SQL and cloud-based data environments
Knowledge of data modelling and KPI development
Experience with AWS data services (e.g. Athena, S3)
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Fluent German language skills




Nice to Have

Experience with railway or operational infrastructure data
Knowledge of Row-Level Security concepts in Power BI
Experience working in agile environments (Scrum / SAFe)




Interested? Please share your CV and availability.




Contract engagement subject to legal and contractual eligibility and local regulations.

Digisourced Ltd acts as an employment business in relation to this opportunity.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
At ING we are looking for a Data Analyst - Financial Crime prevention Tribe







Your role and work environment:




We are looking for a talented and enthusiastic Data Analyst to join our COE Team of the Financial Crime Prevention Tribe.







The responsibility of this team is to drive the end‑to‑end design and continuous improvement of Financial Crime Prevention processes, ensuring that regulatory requirements are translated into clear, scalable standards and controls. By governing process integrity, overseeing evolution and testing, and coordinating across Compliance, Operations, IT and Global stakeholders, the team enables ING to manage financial‑crime risks effectively and deliver consistent, high‑quality CDD execution

Your key responsibilities:




As a Data Analyst you will:




Positively contribute to:

The management and analysis of FCP data, enabling the delivery of key local and global initiatives and regulatory reports by ensuring accurate, complete and timely information.

Co‑create:

FCP data solutions together with the FCP team and other stakeholders (Data Office, Compliance, etc.), acting as a local data expert in both local and global FCP projects.
Data frameworks to understand, define and organize FCP data needs, translating them into actionable data plans.

Ensure

The availability, quality and reliability of FCP data across different initiatives, proactively identifying gaps and data limitations.
The creation, execution and continuous improvement of end‑to‑end processes to obtain, analyze and validate FCP data, working closely with the FCP team in business‑as‑usual activities.




What are we looking for?




Want to know if this job is made for you? These are some of the qualities that we value the most for this role. And if the shoe fits… don’t hesitate to apply!




Knows about:

Data process automatization.

Strong knowledge in SAS, SQL, Excel and Windows environment.

Designing, defining, and developing dashboards and operational Kpi’s




Is a master of:

Adding value to data, obtain relevant insights, go a step further to contribute to strategic decision making and challenge established ones.




Loves to:

Work as a team, taking advantage of his strong communication skills.




Defines himself as:

Flexible, dynamic, capable of working in multidisciplinary environments in coordination with the rest of the areas




What do we offer?




The time you spend at work, the challenges you face or the lessons you get are very important, but… What about your personal life? At ING we want your work to fulfill you in every way, and that is why we take care of even the smallest details.




Check out what is waiting for you!




Be flexible my friend.

Our model is all about flexibility and accountability. Keeping both our customers and our colleagues’ needs in mind, you determine together which days you work at home and which you come to ING MAD to offer your best self. Do your thing.

Restaurant card.

So that thinking about what to have for lunch doesn’t take up your time or your cravings.




Our house will be your home.

In our offices you can find electric mobility solutions, doctor, hairdresser, gym, The Good Service (to help you with your errands) and much more!




Health insurance.

For you and all your family (spouse/partner and children).




Life insurance.

We help you protect what matters most to you.




Flexible remuneration.

In addition, you will enjoy our flexible remuneration model, through a more tax-advantaged way, you will be able to access other services such as nursery, transport card, training aids…




Free company shuttle.

It doesn't matter where you live. We have 6 routes (North, Central, South, A5, A42 and A6) to get you to our ING office in Madrid comfortably.




Banking benefits.

Special loans with more beneficial conditions.

Special mortgage conditions after 6 months working at the bank.

And you can benefit from our pension plan after 1 month with us!




More vitality and more belonging

360º care of the employee (physical, mental, social connection & purpose).

Physiotherapy service on site.

Clinics with professional, world-class athletes.

Gympass service.

Employee Networks: Rainbow Lions (LGBTQI+), Leonas & RIÑG.

International Remote work program.




Get to know Us




We are a team of more than 1800 people sharing one idea: doing our bit in helping our clients create a better future for themselves, by providing them with the tools they need to get where they want to go.




A fully online bank where technology, innovation, simplicity and transparency are the main ingredients, and the first one to work with agile methodologies.




And although we are based in Spain, we are lucky to work in an international bank with presence in more than 40 countries.




We are proud to have a diverse team, and we are committed to continue developing a culture of Diversity, Inclusion and Belonging to foster a culture of collaboration and innovation that represents all collectives in the society.




And even though we look orange on the outside, we are green on the inside. Our ambition in Sustainability is Empowering (y)our Sustainability journey. We are embarking on our path, for us, but we are also there for when our clients decide to embark on this journey.




Making things happen, helping others be successful, always being one step ahead—that’s our DNA. If you look at yourself in the mirror and your reflection matches our way of being and working…we are looking forward to meeting you!




At ING, you belong just as you are. We hire and support people for their talent and potential — and we know that when you can be yourself, you do your best work.




Job Application Safety Reminder




We’re seeing an increase in fraudulent job offers. To protect yourself, please follow these key guidelines when applying for roles at ING:

Apply only via official ING platforms: ING uses Workday and LinkedIn as its internal recruitment system. Applications should be submitted only via our official career site or via ING job postings on LinkedIn.
Check the sender’s email carefully: Legitimate communication will always come from @ing.com and/or @myworkday.com.
No payments or banking details will ever be requested: If someone asks for this information, it’s a scam.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
At Next Geosolutions, we are a leading international marine geoscience and offshore survey company, supporting complex offshore energy and infrastructure projects worldwide. As part of our continued growth, we are looking for talented professionals to join our dynamic and collaborative team. We offer a competitive salary package and the opportunity to work in an innovative, fast-paced international environment.




Next Geosolutions is looking for a hands-on Data Analyst / BI Specialist to support and enhance our business intelligence and data management capabilities across Risk, Lessons Learned, Document Management, and operational datasets.




This role is ideal for a data-driven professional with strong analytical skills, advanced Excel and Power BI expertise, and a passion for transforming complex data into meaningful business insights.




Key Responsibilities

• Consolidate and manage data from multiple sources including Excel, SharePoint, SQL databases, and document systems

• Develop dashboards, reports, and KPIs using Power BI

• Build and maintain data models, workflows, and automated reporting solutions

• Perform quantitative analysis including trend analysis, sensitivity analysis, and Monte Carlo simulations

• Support tendering, commercial activities, and operational decision-making through data insights

• Improve the data quality, consistency, and reporting efficiency across the business




What We’re Looking For

• Extremely advanced Microsoft Excel and Power BI skills

• Strong SQL and data modelling capability

• Experience with statistics, risk analysis, and data visualisation

• Knowledge of Power Query, DAX, and Microsoft 365 environments

• Python, Power Platform, or Microsoft Fabric experience would be an advantage

• Experience within engineering, offshore, marine, construction, or project environments is beneficial




Preferred Background

• Degree in a quantitative discipline such as Engineering, Data Science, Mathematics, Statistics, or Computer Science

• Microsoft Power Platform Solution Architect or Power BI certifications are desirable




We’re looking for someone proactive, detail-oriented, and capable of turning data into actionable business intelligence in a fast-paced environment.




📩 Apply now or get in touch to learn more.




Next Geosolutions provides equal employment opportunity to all individuals regardless of their race, colour, creed, religion, gender, age, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by state, federal or local law.

Title: INTERNSHIP DATA ANALYST
Description:
CRIF, prestigioso ed innovativo Gruppo internazionale specializzato in soluzioni informative, modelli decisionali, outsourcing, software e servizi consulenziali per supportare banche, società finanziarie, assicurazioni, utilities e imprese in ogni fase della relazione con il cliente




ricerca figure di:

INTERNSHIP DATA ANALYST




da inserire nel team Analytics and Advance Analytics Consultancy, con il principale compito di rinforzare e trasferire competenze Advance Analytics. La risorsa avrà l’obiettivo di sviluppare, monitorare, gestire la produzione e analizzare i risultati allo scopo di migliorare sempre i modelli statistici/previsionali e in generale gli analytics in carico alla BU. La posizione prevede diverse attività tra cui:

Gestione e organizzazione dei progetti del team Analytics and Advance Analytics Consultancy.
Predisposizione degli script, utilizzando il linguaggio di scrittura in uso al BU (SQL, Python, SAS, …) per prodotti, servizi e soluzioni.
Supporto al team degli sviluppatori nella gestione e manutenzione IT dei prodotti.
Supporto ai team di prodotto ove le soluzioni analytics della BU vengono innestate.
Proceduralizzazione ed automazione delle attività dell’ufficio.




Il candidato ideale possiede:

Laurea in ambiti quantitativi (economia, statistica, informatica, ingegneria gestionale, fisica o discipline affini)
Appassionato di tematiche Advance Analytics.
Competenze di programmazione con linguaggio SQL- Phyton o SAS
Capacità di trasmettere efficacemente le tematiche Advance Analytics, sia metodologiche, di programmazione/implementative e di business
Capacità di data manipulation e data preparation.
Collaborare con i team per integrare i sistemi.
Competenze in Microsoft PowerPoint e Microsoft Excel (tabelle pivot, comfort con formule avanzate, VBA a plus).
Ottima conoscenza della lingua inglese, sia scritta che parlata (B2).
Forti capacità interpersonali, di collaborazione, leadership e comunicazione.
Capacità di esprimersi in modo chiaro e conciso, sia verbalmente che per iscritto.
Eccellenti capacità analitiche e creative di risoluzione dei problemi.
Attenzione ai dettagli e capacità di definire le priorità e lavorare su più progetti.
Giocatore di squadra con la capacità di adattare il ritmo di lavoro in base alle priorità del cliente (interno ed esterno).
Capacità di lavorare rapidamente ed efficacemente con concetti matematici, modelli e statistiche.




Si offre un ambiente di lavoro dinamico ed in forte crescita e un rimborso spese di 1.100 euro per 6 mensilità.




Work Location: Bologna – Varignana, con possibilità di smart working al 40%




In CRIF, siamo impegnati a coltivare un ambiente sicuro e inclusivo, dove l'innovazione prospera e i colleghi sono incoraggiati a pensare in modo creativo. Ci impegniamo a promuovere pari opportunità durante tutto il ciclo di vita dei dipendenti e crediamo che la diversità di genere, età, cultura, religione e disabilità sia fondamentale per attrarre e trattenere i migliori talenti. Abbracciando queste differenze, miriamo a creare un ambiente di lavoro in cui tutti possano avere successo e contribuire al nostro successo condiviso

La posizione prevede diverse attività tra cui:

  • Gestione e organizzazione dei progetti del team Analytics and Advance Analytics Consultancy.
  • Predisposizione degli script, utilizzando il linguaggio di scrittura in uso al BU (SQL, Python, SAS, …) per prodotti, servizi e soluzioni.
  • Supporto al team degli sviluppatori nella gestione e manutenzione IT dei prodotti.
  • Supporto ai team di prodotto ove le soluzioni analytics della BU vengono innestate.
  • Proceduralizzazione ed automazione delle attività dell’ufficio.


Il candidato ideale possiede:

  • Laurea in ambiti quantitativi (economia, statistica, informatica, ingegneria gestionale, fisica o discipline affini)
  • Appassionato di tematiche Advance Analytics.
  • Competenze di programmazione con linguaggio SQL- Phyton o SAS
  • Capacità di trasmettere efficacemente le tematiche Advance Analytics, sia metodologiche, di programmazione/implementative e di business
  • Capacità di data manipulation e data preparation.
  • Collaborare con i team per integrare i sistemi.
  • Competenze in Microsoft PowerPoint e Microsoft Excel (tabelle pivot, comfort con formule avanzate, VBA a plus).
  • Ottima conoscenza della lingua inglese, sia scritta che parlata (B2).
  • Forti capacità interpersonali, di collaborazione, leadership e comunicazione.
  • Capacità di esprimersi in modo chiaro e conciso, sia verbalmente che per iscritto.
  • Eccellenti capacità analitiche e creative di risoluzione dei problemi.
  • Attenzione ai dettagli e capacità di definire le priorità e lavorare su più progetti.
  • Giocatore di squadra con la capacità di adattare il ritmo di lavoro in base alle priorità del cliente (interno ed esterno).
  • Capacità di lavorare rapidamente ed efficacemente con concetti matematici, modelli e statistiche.


Si offre un ambiente di lavoro dinamico ed in forte crescita e un rimborso spese di 1.100 euro per 6 mensilità.


Work Location: Bologna – Varignana, con possibilità di smart working al 40%


In CRIF, siamo impegnati a coltivare un ambiente sicuro e inclusivo, dove l'innovazione prospera e i colleghi sono incoraggiati a pensare in modo creativo. Ci impegniamo a promuovere pari opportunità durante tutto il ciclo di vita dei dipendenti e crediamo che la diversità di genere, età, cultura, religione e disabilità sia fondamentale per attrarre e trattenere i migliori talenti. Abbracciando queste differenze, miriamo a creare un ambiente di lavoro in cui tutti possano avere successo e contribuire al nostro successo condiviso

Title: Staff Product Data Analyst
Description:
As a Staff Product Data Analyst, you’ll leverage data to drive strategic decision-making and optimize product performance. Success in this position means delivering actionable insights that lead to measurable improvements in product KPIs and overall user satisfaction. This role is integral to our product team, ensuring data-driven decisions guide our product strategy and innovation efforts.

You’ll join the Product Insights department, composed of the Product Data Analytics team and Product Research team. In charge of having a 360 customer knowledge view, we combine quantitative and qualitative insights to inform the product roadmap. You will be responsible to support the Growth product line, focused on making the Product self-serve, from trial to conversion, and partner directly with the Director/VP of Product. You will be reporting to the Data Analytics manager of this team.

What You’ll Do


Identify and establish the core Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) that the product team will focus on to measure product success and impact. Ensure these KPIs align with overall business objectives and provide a clear framework for evaluating product performance.
Guide and empower product teams with advanced methodologies and analytical tools to foster a data-driven culture. Provide training and support to ensure teams can independently leverage data insights in their decision-making processes.
Help shape the experimentation strategy of our fast-paced team by designing A/B tests and testing guidelines, aligning success metrics across cross-functional teams, validating the technical setup, tracking experiment performance, and guiding stakeholders on expected time to insights and interpretation of results.
Conduct thorough analysis of product performance metrics and user behavior to identify trends, issues, and opportunities for improvement. Use these insights to drive strategic decisions that enhance product functionality, user experience, and market competitiveness.
Partner with the Product researcher of your line to create and maintain an overview of the performance insights uncovered throughout the customer journey, identify the customer knowledge gaps that remain and leverage critical thinking to proactively influence the product roadmap discussions.
You’ll partner directly with the Director of Product of the Line you’re covering.


What You’ll Need To Succeed


Proficiency in statistical analysis, data modeling, and data visualization. Ability to interpret complex data sets and translate findings into actionable insights.
Experience with data analysis, data modeling and visualization tools (SQL, DBT, Excel, Tableau, or similar software). Expertise in creating advanced data visualizations that effectively communicate complex insights to a broad audience, including dashboards and interactive reports.
Capacity to identify issues, analyze root causes, and develop effective solutions. Ability to approach problems methodically and think critically. Meticulous in analyzing data and ensuring accuracy. Ability to validate findings thoroughly and maintain high standards in data integrity and reporting.
Ability to convey complex data insights clearly and effectively to non-technical stakeholders. Strong written and verbal communication skills for reporting and presentations.
Experience working in cross-functional teams, including product managers, engineers, and designers. Ability to build relationships and influence stakeholders at all levels of the organization.
Strong organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously. Experience with project management tools and methodologies.
Understanding of business strategy and how data-driven insights can support and enhance product and business goals. Ability to align data analysis with business objectives.
You’ll need to have a prior experience as a data analyst and to hold a business or engineering degree.


What Makes You Stand Out


You have a prior experience using Contentsquare platform.
Understanding of machine learning concepts and experience with implementing predictive models to forecast product performance and user behavior.
Previous experience in product management or working closely with product leaders to understand the full product lifecycle and strategic considerations.
Experience in the specific industry relevant to the company’s products, providing contextual understanding that can enhance the analysis and recommendations.


We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Data Analyst (Quick Commerce)
Description:
As the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience, fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in over 70+ countries worldwide, powered by tech, designed by people. As one of Europe’s largest tech platforms, headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index. We enable creative minds to deliver solutions that create impact within our ecosystem. We move fast, take action and adapt. No matter where you're from or what you believe in, we build, we deliver, we lead. We are Delivery Hero.

Job Description

We are on the lookout for a Data Analyst to join our Data Analytics team within Quick Commerce, on our journey to always deliver amazing experiences.

Be part of redefining how customers experience quick commerce. You’ll help build technology that scales our non-food offerings, reaching new market segments and driving revenue growth. By innovating within our Quick Commerce Team, you’ll make Delivery Hero the go-to platform for a broad range of products, helping us grow faster and deliver more value to customers around the world.

You will partner closely with Product and business teams, driving operational excellence and strategic insights across Fulfillment Operations, Supply Chain, Assortment, Pricing, Promotion and Demand Planning. Focusing on metrics monitoring, deep dive analysis, experimentation, and fostering a data-driven culture. Your insights will directly shape product and business roadmaps, optimize features, and assess impact. You will define KPIs, design experiments, and communicate findings to senior stakeholders. You'll help us ensure product and business initiatives deliver measurable business value, transitioning from a reporting mindset to a problem-solving approach. Your work will directly impact our ability to scale and grow our non-food offerings.

Key Responsibilities:


Collaborate with Product Managers and cross-functional teams to define hypotheses, KPIs, and success criteria
Conduct in-depth analyses of product usage, adoption, and impact metrics to identify trends and opportunities
Establish effective causal inference strategies to evaluate interrelations and identify growth drivers
Support the implementation of tracking strategies and ensure data integrity
Generate business intelligence solutions and compelling visualizations to communicate key insights


Qualifications


Proven 2+ years experience in Product Analytics or Data Analytics, ideally within a fast-paced product-driven environment
Excellent stakeholder management, communication and collaboration skills
Strong technical expertise in analytics stack including SQL, dbt, Airflow, and Python, with proficiency in visualization tools (Tableau, Looker Studio) and experience with A/B testing platforms (Optimizely, Adobe Target)
Solid understanding of statistical concepts, experimental design, A/B testing methodologies and Quasi Experimentation
Experience with event tracking and instrumentation tools
Ability to work autonomously and navigate through uncertainty


Additional Information

Ensuring you and all our Heroes are looked after, happy, and healthy is always on the menu. Because if you’re in good shape, then we’re in good shape.


Make the most of our hybrid working model and join the team for face-to-face connection and collaboration in our beautiful Berlin campus 2 days a week
We offer 27 days holiday with an extra day on 2nd and 3rd year of service
We will support you in developing yourself and your career growth opportunities: 1.000 € Educational Budget, Language Courses, Parental Support and access to the Udemy Business platform to explore a variety of online courses.
Get moving and release those wonderful, mind-boosting endorphins: Health Checkups, Meditation, Yoga, Gym & Bicycle Subsidy
Cash. Dough. Cheddar. Whatever you call it, we’ll help you with it: Employee Share Purchase Plan, Sabbatical Bank, Public Transportation Ticket Discount, Life & Accident Insurance, Corporate Pension Plan
The power of getting together over some food is unrivaled. Here are a few ways to help you do that. All the yum: Digital Meal Vouchers, Food Vouchers, Corporate Discounts. Courses.


Ready to join our team? If you’re excited to grow, collaborate and be part of the world’s leading delivery platform, we’d love to hear from you. Apply today!

We believe diversity and inclusion are key to creating not only an exciting product, but also an amazing customer and employee experience. Fostering this starts with hiring - therefore we do not discriminate on the basis of racial identities, religious beliefs, color, national origin, gender identities or expressions, sexual orientations, age, marital or disability statuses, or any other aspect that makes you, you.

We encourage you to let us know if you need any accommodations or specific accessibility support to ensure a smooth interview experience—just let us know with an email to our Inclusion Officer at inclusion@deliveryhero.com.

Severely disabled applicants with equal qualifications will be given preferential consideration.

You're welcome to share your pronouns (he/she/they) right from the start so we can address you respectfully from our first contact.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Company Description

Inetum is a global leader in IT services, dedicated to delivering innovative solutions to our clients. We are committed to fostering a dynamic and inclusive work environment that values diversity, where creativity and collaboration can thrive. We are present in 19 countries and have more than 28,000 employees worldwide.

If you are looking for a dynamic, innovative, and technology-driven company, Inetum is the place for you! Come be part of Inetum!

Job Description


Identify business problems and propose data-driven solutions to support decision-making and growth
Retrieve, clean, prepare, and analyze complex datasets from multiple sources
Design and implement analyses using statistical methods and data models
Develop reports, dashboards, and visualizations following best practices (Tableau, Power BI)
Build automated tools and solutions to deliver recurring insights efficiently
Contribute to data feed industrialization, ETL pipelines, and data warehousing processes
Translate business requirements into technical specifications for the development team
Communicate complex analytical results clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Collaborate closely with traders, sales teams, business sponsors, and technical teams


Qualifications


Academic background in Engineering, Economics, Computer Science, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related field
Professional experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau and/or Power BI
Professional experience in data analysis using programming languages, preferably Python (Pandas)
Strong ability to analyze, synthesize, and simplify complex information
Knowledge of descriptive statistics and hypothesis testing is a plus
Familiarity with Big Data technologies such as PySpark, Presto, and Airflow is a plus
Knowledge of Power Query and DAX is a plus
Fluent in English

Title: Data Analyst en alternance (H/F)
Description:
À la recherche d’une expérience au service d’une marque patrimoniale, engagée et audacieuse ?

Rejoignez Nature & Découvertes !




Chez Nature & Découvertes, nous sommes convaincus que le vivant est un cadeau.




Portés par la passion et par nos convictions, nous avons fait le pari singulier, dès notre création en 1990, de faire du commerce dans le respect de l’humain et de la nature. Depuis, ce sont nos actions qui le prouvent : 150 projets associatifs soutenus par notre Fondation chaque année, notre certification B Corp, obtenue dès 2015, et notre statut de société à mission, depuis fin 2022.




Nos univers phares - bien-être, outdoor, maison, jeux-jouets et librairie - sont composés d’une sélection de produits exigeante, singulière et experte.




Avec plus de 90 magasins en Europe et près de 900 collaborateurs, nous nous engageons également à accompagner le développement des équipes, notamment grâce à notre école de formation interne.




Au sein de l’équipe Data Analytics de Nature & Découvertes, nous recherchons une alternance en Data Analyst pour nous aider à traiter l’ensemble des données disponibles, afin de développer la connaissance de nos clients dans l’entreprise, suivre et optimiser l’efficacité des actions, développer et maintenir des outils innovants pour exploiter la data clients.




• Management et Structuration de la donnée

- Nous faisons régulièrement évoluer notre modèle de données afin de couvrir l’ensemble des besoins analytics de Nature & Découvertes (structuration, optimisation et intégration de nouvelles données).- Vous participez à la définition du modèle et des indicateurs pour garantir la fiabilité des

données.




• Reportings et Etudes clients

- Vous avez en charge l’élaboration des reportings et les faites évoluer.

- Vous analysez les résultats et les mettez en forme via des techniques de visualisation.

- Vous mesurez la performance des opérations marketing mises en place (A/B test, Offres commerciales …)

- Vous participez à la réalisation d’études sur les comportements clients (omnicanalité, activité, churn …)




• Modélisation data

- Vous travaillerez sur au moins un projet de modélisation data dans l’outil DATAIKU.

- Vous intégrerez l’équipe data analytics composée de 1 personne.




Environnement : Hadoop / logiciels utilisés (BI : MicroStrategy) Dataiku/ Python

ProfilBac +4/5, école de statistiques ou ingénieur spécialisée en data

- Vous savez manipuler les bases de données.*

- Vous connaissez SQL et Python

- Vous avez déjà utilisé Excel et Power Point

- Vous connaissez les principales techniques de modélisation (segmentation, scoring, associations de produits …)




Qualités souhaitées :




Nous privilégions la co-création et le travail en équipe. Nous espérons que vous aussi !

Vous êtes une personne curieuse, polyvalente, vous aimez travailler sur des problématiques variées. Vous faites preuve d’autonomie et êtes force de proposition.

Vous êtes une personne rigoureuse et vous avez le sens du détail !




Aussi, vous avez une sensibilité marketing et des capacités de communication écrite et orale

Poste basé sur notre Siège Social de Versailles (78), situé face à la gare de Versailles Chantiers (liaison RER C et Ligne N). Accord télétravail et mobilité durable.)

Remboursement des frais de transport à hauteur de 67%, carte tickets restaurants et accès aux avantages du CSE.

Title: Data Labelling Analyst
Description:
Data Labelling Analyst

📍 Fully Onsite

🕘 Monday–Friday | 9:00am–6:00pm

📅 11-Month Contract (with potential extension)

💰 €73,000 per annum (pro rata)




Our client — a global leader in technology and digital communication — is seeking detail-oriented and analytical Data Labelling Analysts to support a high-impact Integrity Operations project focused on online safety and user protection.




This is an opportunity to work at the forefront of AI training and integrity review, helping improve how technology identifies harmful behaviour, unwanted contact, scams, impersonation, and other safety-related risks across digital platforms.




What You’ll Be Doing

Reviewing and annotating content to support AI model training and evaluation
Applying policy reasoning to make accurate, consistent integrity decisions

Title: Data Analyst (Insurance Sector)
Description:
For an innovative organization active within the insurance and financial services sector, we are looking for a Data Analyst. In this position, you will play a key role in transforming complex data into actionable insights that support strategic and operational decision-making across the business.

You will work in a data-driven environment where analytics, reporting, and continuous improvement are central. The role combines technical expertise with strong stakeholder interaction and offers the opportunity to contribute to both business performance and process optimization.



Your Responsibilities



Data Management & Preparation


Collect and consolidate data from multiple internal and external sources
Clean, validate, and structure datasets to ensure data quality and consistency
Support the maintenance and optimization of reporting datasets and analytical environments


Data Analysis & Insights


Analyze large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and business opportunities
Perform statistical and exploratory data analyses
Translate complex data into clear business insights and recommendations
Support business teams with ad hoc analyses and reporting requests


Reporting & Visualization


Develop dashboards, reports, and visualizations using modern BI tools
Present data in a clear and business-friendly way for both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Monitor KPIs and business performance metrics


Stakeholder Collaboration


Work closely with business, operational, and IT teams to understand reporting and analytical needs
Support decision-making through data-driven recommendations
Participate in initiatives focused on process improvement and data quality enhancement


Continuous Improvement


Stay informed about new technologies, tools, and best practices in analytics and BI
Contribute to the evolution of the organization's data capabilities and reporting standards
Share knowledge and collaborate within the broader data community


Your Profile


Experience in Data Analysis, Reporting, BI, or a similar analytical role
Strong knowledge of SQL for querying and manipulating data
Experience with reporting and visualization tools such as Power BI, Tableau, or similar
Familiarity with Python or R is considered a strong asset
Understanding of data modeling and data warehousing concepts
Strong analytical mindset with excellent problem-solving skills
Able to communicate complex insights in a clear and structured way
Autonomous, detail-oriented, and proactive
Bachelor's or Master's degree in a relevant field such as Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or similar
Professional level of English and fluent in either Dutch or French


What's Offered


The opportunity to join a modern and data-driven organization
A collaborative environment where innovation and analytics are highly valued
Exposure to large-scale data and impactful business projects
Flexibility, autonomy, and opportunities for professional growth
Competitive salary package with additional benefits

Title: DATA ANALYST
HTML:
CRIF, azienda globale leader nei sistemi di informazioni creditizie, business information, analytics, outsourcing e soluzioni digitali avanzate per lo sviluppo del business e l’open banking, ricerca un


Data analyst


La risorsa si occuperà di collaborare alle attività di Data Management e Data Elaboration nell’ambito del servizio Legal Eye, per Cribis credit management, società del gruppo.


Le attività :


  • elaborazione di informazioni strutturate e non strutturate, combinando metadati automatici e strumenti di Intelligenza Artificiale, al fine di fornire: servizi di arricchimento informativo; dashboard di monitoraggio e benchmarking; supporto decisionale tramite modelli di predictive justice.
  • Partecipazione al piano di evoluzione e digitalizzazione della Practice, contribuendo al test e allo sviluppo di nuove soluzioni basate su Intelligenza Artificiale, finalizzate all’automazione e all’efficientamento dei processi di credit management.
  • Collaborare in modo continuativo con: l’area Operations, per la raccolta e la comprensione dei requisiti di business e per le attività di testing delle soluzioni; l’area IT, per lo sviluppo e l’implementazione delle soluzioni nel rispetto delle linee guida aziendali.
  • Spiccate capacità di teamwork e di collaborazione interfunzionale;
  • Attitudine alla comprensione di processi di business;
  • Capacità analitica unita a un approccio proattivo e orientato alla ricerca di soluzioni innovative.


REQUISTI

  • Familiarità con strumenti emergenti di Intelligenza Artificiale (es. LLM, strumenti di AI-assisted analytics) e interesse ad approfondirne l’utilizzo in contesti di automazione e supporto decisionale
  • Laurea in discipline informatiche, ingegneristiche o statistiche, oppure esperienza professionale equivalente;
  • Buona conoscenza di MS Office, SQL e API REST o altri linguaggi di programmazione;
  • Costituisce titolo preferenziale la conoscenza del linguaggio Python;
  • Almeno un anno di esperienza lavorativa, maturata in CRIF o in contesti analoghi.
  • Buona conoscenza della lingua inglese sia scritta sia parlata;
  • Conoscenza madrelingua della lingua italiana.


sede di lavoro: Bologna

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
This isn’t your regular job. Almedia is a place where those who want to push harder can accelerate their careers faster than anywhere else. We’re aiming to become Germany’s second bootstrapped unicorn. Almedia is already Europe’s #3 fastest-growing company in 2025 (FT1000).

We are building the future of marketing by rewarding our community of over 70 million users for engaging with our advertisers’ products. We are offering a new way to acquire users for the biggest companies in the world.

We are seeking a Data Analyst with a passion for uncovering insights that drive growth. This pivotal role is for a highly analytical individual who will directly influence our product, rewards, marketing, and user acquisition strategies through deep dives into user behaviour and advanced predictive modelling.

Join us and become a central force in optimising our user acquisition and incentivisation, for maximising Lifetime Value (LTV) for millions of users.

What You'll Do 🎯


Focus on Causal Analysis: Quantify the causal impact of monetary incentives and product features on user retention. Identify the key behavioural drivers that lead to long-term LTV growth across Freecash and Link.
Design, Run and Analyse A/B tests: Own the end-to-end experimentation lifecycle (design, power analysis, execution, and interpretation). Use A/B and multivariate testing to validate causal hypotheses and drive measurable incremental growth, across different domains (product, rewards, UA) to drive incremental outcomes for business top line and bottom line.
Drive Predictive Analytics: Focus on analysing user behaviour and marketing activities to predict key metrics like Return on Ad Spend, identifying high-value user segments and opportunities for growth.
Deep User Segmentation: Lead in developing and applying advanced user segmentation models, providing actionable insights that inform product development, marketing strategies, and personalised user experiences.
Performance Optimisation & Strategy: Partner closely with Engineering, Product, and User Acquisition teams to interpret complex performance data, translate findings into strategic recommendations, and directly influence optimisation efforts.
Advanced Data Analysis & Modelling: Utilise strong SQL and Python skills to conduct sophisticated data analysis, build robust analytical models, and maintain relevant data structures that support predictive insights.
Insights Communication & Enablement: Transform complex analytical findings into clear, compelling narratives and actionable recommendations for stakeholders. Mentor and train teams on how to leverage these insights effectively, moving beyond basic reporting.


What You'll Bring 💡


Extensive experience (typically 4+ years) in a data analysis role with a strong focus on business performance, user behaviour, and growth metrics.
Proven experience with technical data analysis, statistics, A/B testing, data modelling and evaluation of algorithmic data solutions.
Exceptional proficiency in SQL and Python for complex data manipulation, analysis, and statistical modelling.
Strong understanding of data warehousing concepts and data modelling principles relevant to analytical outcomes.
Experience in collaborating closely with marketing, product, and data science teams to drive measurable business impact.
Ability to communicate complex analytical findings clearly and concisely to diverse audiences, influencing data-driven decisions.


What Makes You a Great Fit 🌟


A Predictive Mindset: You are passionate about uncovering future trends and user behaviour through data, using insights to drive proactive strategies.
Impact-Driven & Strategic: You're not just reporting numbers; you're using them to influence key business decisions and directly contribute to revenue and user growth.
Collaborative Analytical Partner: You excel at translating complex data into actionable strategies for business stakeholders, thriving in a cross-functional environment.


Why Almedia? 🎁


Own Our Growth: We offer all Berlin-based employees equity in Almedia to truly be a part of our success.
Scale With Almedia: Grow alongside a startup that has been profitable from day one.
Central Berlin Office: Work from a fully-stocked modern office built for collaboration, accessible from all around Berlin.
Other Benefits: Transport subsidy, breakfasts and lunches, language learning, Urban Sports Club, and more.
We Listen: We regularly add to our benefits through rigorous employee feedback.


We believe in fostering talent, evaluating all skill levels during the hiring process, and providing a clear path for growth. Almedia is an equal opportunity employer. We embrace and celebrate diversity, and encourage individuals from all backgrounds to apply.

Title: Data Analyst (Product)
Description:
About Revolut

People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 70+ million customers get more from their money every day.

As we continue our lightning-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution.

About The Role

Our Data Science team solves complex problems with smart, practical solutions. Data Analysts and Scientists ‌work directly with product teams to uncover insights, guide decisions, and improve how customers experience Revolut. They do this by delivering smart, scalable solutions that move the business forward.

We’re looking for a talented Data Analyst to dig into our complex databases, look for root causes of a problem, design your own solutions, and write your own code to implement them. We aim for you to never stop learning, picking up new skills and delivering value along the way.

Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch.

What You’ll Be Doing


Understanding our business and its processes through our data
Applying this understanding and knowledge of data to help product and service teams
Designing key metrics to measure different aspects of the business
Creating and maintaining new aggregated views and tables to simplify data querying
Maintaining and creating new dashboards to track metrics and visualise insights
Promoting data literacy across the company, organising and holding workshops


What You'll Need


Previous experience in an analytical role, creating impactful solutions
A background/education in a quantitative discipline
5+ years of experience with SQL
Great skills with Python or other programming languages
Evidence of solid mathematical and statistics knowledge


Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard-working team.

Important notice for candidates:

Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles.


Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third-party services or platforms for our recruitment.
Always double-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain.


We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process. If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately.

By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice
