Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Department: Finance

Employment Type: Permanent

Location: Burlington, Massachusetts

Compensation: $100,000 - $110,000 / year

Description

Highwire, a Veriforce company, is the Contractor Success Platform for asset owners, general contractors, and facilities managers. The world’s most admired organizations use Highwire to ensure their contractors and subcontractors deliver great work, on budget and on schedule, by collaborating with them to dynamically mitigate safety, financial, quality, and sustainability risks during capital projects and facilities maintenance.

To support our growth, we’re looking for a technically minded, detail-obsessed Data Analyst to own the integrity of our internal data. This role is perfect for someone who’s confident with SQL and eager to work directly with complex datasets.

Your mission is to ensure the data that powers Highwire — from customer renewal rates and campaign performance to financial reporting — is accurate, complete, and decision-ready. You’ll take ownership of the end-to-end accuracy of key datasets, using SQL to investigate anomalies, validate transformations, and deliver reliable data to teams across Product, Engineering, Finance, and other business functions. Your work will enable the entire company to move quickly and make informed decisions based on trustworthy data.

What You'll Do


Write and improve SQL queries that power reporting and analysis. You’ll support business teams by extracting and transforming data, building reusable queries, and collaborating with engineering to enhance data models and pipelines.
Deliver clean, trusted data. You’ll ensure that the data used across Finance, Product, Sales, and Engineering is accurate and reliable, enabling fast and confident decision-making and reducing rework and second-guessing.
Identify and fix root-cause issues. You’ll proactively identify bugs, inconsistencies, or oddities in the data that powers our reports, and collaborate with Engineering and Product to determine the root cause of the issue.
Enable real-time reporting. Department heads will rely on you for recurring and ad-hoc reports that are both timely and accurate. You’ll build reports that scale as our teams grow.
Continuously improve data quality. You won’t wait for others to flag data issues. Instead, you’ll take initiative to audit, investigate, and improve datasets before problems reach stakeholders.


Who You Are


Confident with SQL and eager to grow. You’re comfortable querying relational databases, navigating schemas, writing joins, and thinking in data logic.
Detail-oriented. You notice inconsistencies that others miss and take pride in spotting the one broken row in a dataset of thousands.
Owner’s mindset. You feel a strong sense of responsibility for the quality of your work and the integrity of the data others rely on. If it’s broken, you’ll fix it.
Adaptable. You’re comfortable working with imperfect or incomplete data, and you adjust quickly to changing product requirements or business needs.
Analytical. You excel at analyzing large datasets, identifying patterns and anomalies, and transforming complex or ambiguous information into actionable insights.
Proactive collaborator. You don’t wait for someone else to raise a flag; you pull in the right people to get the right answer, fast.


This is an ideal role for someone who is confident in writing SQL to explore, transform, and troubleshoot data in a relational database. You might come from a background in data analysis, analytics engineering, operational support, or even Excel/Sheets-heavy reporting work, but the key is that you’ve used SQL hands-on, and you’re excited to deepen that skill and partner closely with leaders across our organization to ensure our data is accurate, accessible, and trustworthy.

Attention to detail is a critical competency for success in this Data Analyst role. To help us identify candidates who read thoroughly, you’ll see a mystery question during the application process. The correct answer is: attention to detail.

What You’ll Get In Return

Our benefits go well beyond the basics. To support your work-life integration, we built a benefits program that takes care of the whole person, including physical, mental, financial, and emotional health.


Medical, Dental, & Vision Insurance. We offer health, dental, and vision insurance to all full-time employees. Best part? You’ll be eligible on day one.
401k. We offer a 401k and a 3% company contribution (even better than a match) to help you get ready to retire no matter how far off that seems.
Parental leave. We take care of new parents by offering paid time off for births, adoptions, and fostering.
Flexible PTO. Our culture of trust and measurement means there’s no limit on time off, just an expectation of performance.
Mentorship. Our office-based culture fosters meaningful learning, growth, and mentorship for our team.

  • 401k. We offer a 401k and a 3% company contribution (even better than a match) to help you get ready to retire no matter how far off that seems.
  • Parental leave. We take care of new parents by offering paid time off for births, adoptions, and fostering.
  • Flexible PTO. Our culture of trust and measurement means there’s no limit on time off, just an expectation of performance.
  • Mentorship. Our office-based culture fosters meaningful learning, growth, and mentorship for our team.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Us

RTK Tickets is a dynamic and rapidly expanding company at the intersection of entertainment, technology, and trading. From buzzer-beaters and sold-out stadiums to front-row concerts, we provide a comprehensive platform for buying, selling, and optimizing access to live events — and increasingly, other high-demand assets.




We’re a fast-moving, data-driven brokerage redefining how markets are priced, traded, and optimized across entertainment and emerging asset classes. What began as a two-person idea has evolved into a 20-person team built on passion, precision, and performance.




Now, we’re looking for a Data Analyst to help us build and scale the data infrastructure and predictive models that power how we buy and sell tickets. If you’re excited by real-time markets, complex datasets, and turning raw data into measurable outcomes, you could be a great fit for our team.




What You’ll Do

As a Data Analyst at RTK Tickets, you’ll own the systems and models that drive our pricing decisions. You’ll design and maintain data pipelines that ingest market data from dozens of sources, build predictive models that forecast demand, and work closely with our risk and operations teams to turn insights into actions.

You’ll have end-to-end ownership; from raw data ingestion to production-ready models. Your work will directly influence how we price, buy, and sell across thousands of live events.







Your Day-to-Day

Design, build, and maintain data pipelines that ingest and process transaction and listing data from external marketplaces
Develop and refine predictive models that forecast demand, identify pricing inefficiencies, and optimize offers
Implement data quality frameworks and monitoring systems to ensure fresh, accurate, and reliable data
Build and manage data warehouse pipelines and modeling (ETL/ELT) for large-scale datasets
Collaborate with the risk and operations teams to translate model outputs into actionable decisions
Partner with engineers to integrate pipelines with CI/CD processes and automate data workflows
Present your findings clearly to both technical and non-technical teammates







What You Bring

Required:

1-3 years of experience in data science, data engineering, or a closely related analytical role
Strong Proficiency in Python - including experience with data processing libraries
Solid SQL skills for data analysis, transformation, and pipeline work
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Applied Mathematics, Statistics, or a related field
Comfortable working in a fast-paced, startup-like environment where you take ownership and adapt quickly
Clear communication skills — able to explain complex ideas simply and confidently
Excitement about AI, automation, and cutting-edge technology

Nice to Have:

Exposure to machine learning model deployment or MLOps practices
Interest in live event markets, trading markets, sports analytics, or alternative asset pricing
Experience with LLM-based automation tools for data extraction or enrichment







Why You’ll Love It Here

At RTK Tickets, you’ll gain hands-on experience at the intersection of data, product, and live entertainment. Your pipelines and models will directly influence how we price and trade across thousands of live events every week.

If you want to grow your career at the intersection of data, markets, and live entertainment, we’d love to hear from you.







Interview Process

Our interview process includes several stages designed to assess both technical skills and team fit. Candidates should be prepared to meet with the hiring team for introductory conversations, followed by a Python-based take-home exercise focused on data ingestion, pipeline design, and predictive modeling - mirroring the kind of end-to-end work this role owns. Candidates will walk through their solution with the team before final interviews with the hiring manager and senior leadership.




Compensation & Benefits

Competitive salary with opportunity for performance-based bonuses
Health and Dental insurance
401(k) retirement plan with company matching
Tickets to select live events

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Worldly Partners: Worldly Partners studies and invests in businesses that we believe will compound capital over decades. Our detailed work focuses on understanding the characteristics of companies that produce very long-term returns and the people who build them.




Job Overview: This role involves working closely with the founder of Worldly Partners to transform complex datasets into actionable insights with the goal of driving very long-term performance and risk management.




Key Responsibilities:

Analyze large, complex financial datasets (market data, alternative data, fundamentals) to identify trends, anomalies, and investment opportunities
Collaborate with investment teams to define data needs and translate them into analytical solutions
Perform exploratory data analysis (EDA) to uncover predictive signals and inefficiencies in markets
Build and maintain data pipelines for sourcing, cleaning, and validating structured and unstructured data
Monitor data quality and integrity across multiple sources
Develop dashboards, reports, and visualizations of data for portfolio monitoring
Automate recurring data processes to improve efficiency and scalability
Stay current with new data sources, tools, and technologies relevant to financial markets
In person data collection




Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or related field
2 – 10+ years of experience in data analysis, preferably in finance, asset management, or trading environments
Experience with data visualization tools (Tableau, Power BI, or similar)
Strong programming skills in Python or SQL, with Java experience preferred
Familiarity with machine learning techniques and model validation practices
Solid understanding of financial markets, instruments, and investment concepts
Experience working with time-series data and large datasets
Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail
Willingness and ability to travel up to 40%




Working Hours:

40 hours per week on site in Needham, MA
Occasional evening or weekend hours may be required depending on business needs
Travel required




Compensation:

$185,000-$225,000
Medical, dental, and vision insurance 100% employer-paid

Title: Data Analyst, Reporting & Analytics
Description:
About Us

National Grid is hiring a Data Analyst (Management Data Analysis), for our Data Analysis and Reporting department in Upstate, NY / Downstate, NY or Waltham, NE.

Everyday we deliver safe and secure energy to homes, communities, and businesses. We are there when people need us the most. We connect people to the energy they need for the lives they live. The pace of change in society and our industry is accelerating and our expertise and track record puts us in an unparalleled position to shape the sustainable future of our industry.

To be successful we must anticipate the needs of our customers, reducing the cost of energy delivery today and pioneering the flexible energy systems of tomorrow. This requires us to deliver on our promises and always look for new opportunities to grow, both ourselves and our business

Job Purpose

The Reporting Analyst plays a critical role in transforming raw enterprise data into trusted, actionable insights that drive operational, financial, and strategic decision‑making. This role is responsible for the full reporting lifecycle — from gathering reporting specifications, validating source‑of‑record datasets, and assessing data quality, to designing intuitive visualizations and maintaining an accurate reporting catalog.

This role also functions as the Product Owner for the Snowflake curated data layer, owning prioritization, acceptance criteria, and delivery of features that supported reliable, consumable data assets.

You will partner closely with business stakeholders, data engineers, data governance, and product teams to ensure reporting assets are accurate, compliant, scalable, and aligned with enterprise data management principles. This role requires strong analytical rigor, technical fluency, and a commitment to continuous learning in modern reporting and data engineering tools.

Key Accountabilities

Product Owner — Curated Data Layer (Snowflake)


Act as Product Owner for the Snowflake curated data layer, owning the product vision, roadmap, and backlog to ensure delivery of high-value, consumable data assets.
Engage business and technical stakeholders to capture needs, translate them into clear user stories and acceptance criteria, and define success metrics and adoption goals.
Prioritize features and enhancements based on business value, dependencies, and risk; maintain a groomed backlog and release plan.
Define and document data contracts, acceptance criteria, SLAs and release acceptance requirements in alignment with enterprise standards; ensure items are catalogued for traceability and audit readiness.
Coordinate work across data engineering, QA, analytics, and data governance to ensure correct implementation, testing, lineage, and production readiness.
Monitor product performance and stakeholder feedback, iterate on requirements and backlog, and remove blockers or escalate impediments as needed.


Reporting Requirements & Specification Management


Engage business stakeholders to capture reporting requirements, KPIs, definitions, and intended decision pathways.
Translate business needs into clear, structured reporting specifications including data sources, logic, filters, metrics, and visualization expectations.
Document specifications in alignment with enterprise standards and ensure they are cataloged for traceability and audit readiness.
Partner with data governance to validate metric definitions, business rules, and approved sources of record.
Optimize report performance, usability, and accessibility for diverse business audiences.
Develop reusable semantic models, datasets, and dataflows to support scalable self‑service analytics.


Data Acquisition, Validation & Quality Assessment


Collaborate with data engineering teams to identify, validate, and acquire the correct source‑of‑record datasets from Snowflake, Azure, Databricks, Salesforce, Maximo, and other enterprise platforms.
Perform data profiling, lineage validation, and quality checks using tools such as Informatica IDQ/EDC, Collibra, Kingsway Soft, SSIS, or Matillion.
Investigate anomalies, reconcile discrepancies, and escalate data quality issues with clear root‑cause analysis.
Ensure all datasets used for reporting adhere to enterprise data management and governance standards.


Reporting Catalog Stewardship


Maintain an accurate, up‑to‑date reporting catalog including report purpose, owners, refresh cadence, data sources, and business definitions.
Ensure catalog entries are complete, relevant, and written in business‑friendly language.
Partner with data governance to ensure catalog metadata aligns with enterprise standards and lineage requirements.


Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in Information Systems, Computer Science, Data Analytics, Engineering, Finance, or related field.
3–7 years of experience in reporting, analytics, or data management roles.
Strong proficiency in SQL (Snowflake SQL, T‑SQL, or equivalent).
Experience with Power BI, Tableau, Looker, or similar BI tools.
Familiarity with Snowflake, Azure Data Services, Databricks, or equivalent cloud data platforms.
Experience with data quality and metadata tools (Informatica IDQ/EDC, Collibra).
Ability to interpret business requirements and translate them into technical specifications.
Strong understanding of data modeling, KPI design, and visualization best practices.
Ability to investigate data anomalies and perform root‑cause analysis.
Excellent communication skills with both technical and business audiences.
Experience working in Agile environments (Jira, Confluence).


More Information

Salary

Upstate, NY $75,000 - $88,000

New England $85,000 - $99,000

Downstate, NY $91,000 - 106,000 a year

National Grid utilizes an assessment that evaluates the job qualifications/characteristics using AI or statistically based scoring. For more information, please view NYC Local Law 144.

This position has a career path which provides for advancement opportunities within and across bands as you develop and evolve in the position; gaining experience, expertise and acquiring and applying technical skills. Candidates will be assessed and provided offers against the minimum qualifications of this role and their individual experience.

National Grid is committed to providing equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment regardless of protected class. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, gender identity, and sexual orientation), national origin, age, disability, genetic information, or any other protected status in accordance with applicable federal, state, and local laws. National Grid maintains affirmative action programs for individuals with disabilities and protected veterans.

Our employment practices are designed to ensure that all individuals are treated fairly and with respect throughout the hiring process and during employment. National Grid complies with all applicable federal, state, and local anti-discrimination laws. We are dedicated to fostering a workplace that is free from unlawful discrimination and harassment, and we encourage a culture of respect for all.

Title: Data Analyst Intern
Description:
Ascensus is the leading independent technology and service platform powering savings plans across America, providing products and expertise that help nearly 16 million people save for a better today and tomorrow.

Are you a student looking to make extra money this summer while gaining valuable work experience? Ascensus is the leading independent technology and service platform powering savings plans across America, providing products and expertise that help nearly 16 million people save for a better today and tomorrow. Our internship program offers practical corporate work experience that could lead to full-time work after graduation. We are seeking students going into their senior year, graduating in the spring of 2027.

The shift is from 9 am to 5 pm, 40 hours per week, starting in June and ending in August. This is a hybrid position in Newton, MA that pays $20 per hour.

The Data Analyst Intern will support the Retirement Operations team by helping transform data into meaningful insights that inform business decisions. This internship is designed to provide hands‑on experience working with real‑world datasets, analytics tools, and business partners while building foundational skills in data analysis, reporting, and data storytelling. The intern will work closely with experienced analysts and stakeholders and receive mentorship throughout the program.

Key Responsibilities


Assist in collecting, cleaning, and validating data from internal and external sources to ensure data quality and accuracy
Support analysis of datasets to identify trends, patterns, and opportunities for operational or business improvement
Help build and maintain dashboards, reports, and visualizations used by leadership and cross‑functional teams
Partner with analysts and business stakeholders to understand data needs and help define metrics and KPIs
Contribute to the development and documentation of data models, data dictionaries, and standard reporting definitions
Perform ad hoc analyses to support business questions and ongoing initiatives
Assist with tracking performance metrics and supporting regular reporting cycles
Collaborate with data engineering, IT, and analytics teams to learn how data pipelines and infrastructure are managed
Follow established data governance, privacy, and security standards


Skills & Qualifications

Required:


Currently pursuing a Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, or a related field (or recent graduate)
Strong interest in data analysis, analytics, or business intelligence
Exposure to SQL or relational databases through coursework, projects, or internships
Familiarity with Excel (basic to intermediate formulas, pivot tables, data analysis concepts)
Ability to analyze data, think critically, and solve problems
Strong attention to detail and willingness to learn
Ability to communicate findings clearly to both technical and non‑technical audiences


Preferred:


Exposure to data visualization tools such as Power BI, Tableau, or Alteryx
Coursework or project experience using Python or R for data analysis
Familiarity with basic data modeling or data warehousing concepts
Interest in financial services, retirement plans, or a highly metrics‑driven industry
Experience working on academic, personal, or team data projects


Core Competencies


Analytical and quantitative thinking
Curiosity and eagerness to learn
Data visualization and storytelling fundamentals
Collaboration and communication skills
Time management and ability to balance multiple priorities
Growth mindset and openness to feedback


Be aware of employment fraud. All email communications from Ascensus or its hiring managers originate from @ascensus.com or @futureplan.com email addresses. We will never ask you for payment or require you to purchase any equipment. If you are suspicious or unsure about validity of a job posting, we strongly encourage you to apply directly through our website.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Site: The General Hospital Corporation

Mass General Brigham relies on a wide range of professionals, including doctors, nurses, business people, tech experts, researchers, and systems analysts to advance our mission. As a not-for-profit, we support patient care, research, teaching, and community service, striving to provide exceptional care. We believe that high-performing teams drive groundbreaking medical discoveries and invite all applicants to join us and experience what it means to be part of Mass General Brigham.

Job Summary

Summary- Collaborates with team members to assist in collecting, analyzing, and interpreting data sets.

Essential Functions-Writes And Runs Basic Queries As Needed.


Assists in maintaining existing reporting systems.
Contributes to presentations to key stakeholders.
Works with senior analysts to design, maintain, and distribute reports.
Learn leading practice analysis methodologies.
Conduct continuous analysis of current performance and accurately forecast data trends.


Qualifications

Education

Bachelor's degree in a related field of study required

Master's degree preferred but not required (MS, MA, or MPH), preferably focusing on applied mathematics, decision science, data science, biostatistics, statistics, computer science, computational biology and/or economics, with a strong background in quantitative coursework and research.

Experience

Minimum 2 years of research experience (inclusive of graduate studies) in the fields of simulation modeling, statistical analysis, econometrics, health policy, computer science, or related field.

Knowledge, Skills And Abilities


Building proficiency in statistics, data analysis, and data sets.
Proficient in time management and project management skills.
Ability to exercise sound judgment to make decisions within the scope of job responsibilities.
Demonstrated knowledge of and skill in word processing, spreadsheet, and database PC applications.
Solid quantitative, analytical, and technical aptitude skills.
Participate in dealing with all levels of employees /management and building proficient relationships with teams and all organizational customers.
Able to find and define problems, understand business impact, identify solutions, and provide recommendations for corrective action.


Additional Job Details (if Applicable)

The Medical Practice Evaluation Center (MPEC) has an opportunity for an enthusiastic and energetic individual to join our research team investigating the clinical and economic value of strategies for the prevention and treatment of diabetes in low- and middle income counties (LIMCs). The candidate should be highly motivated with experience in mathematics, statistics, and/or computer science. Ideally, the candidate will have experience in numerical methods, Bayesian statistics, and/or mathematical model development and parameterization. Some experience using general purpose programming languages is also a plus. The position offers a stimulating, collaborative, and multidisciplinary environment and the opportunity to contribute to the development of several NIH-funded disease models. The primary responsibilities of this individual will be to help construct and parameterize model-based cost-effectiveness analyses using a range of data sources, lead in abstract presentation and manuscript preparation, assist with model refinement and expansion, and assist with new proposals and grant preparation. There are multiple possibilities for collaboration with other researchers in Boston, as well as with many national and international research groups and institutions.

For more information about the position and MPEC, please visit https://mpec.massgeneral.org/career-opportunities/#diabetesda

Construct and parameterize disease simulation models with individual and population-level data using advanced mathematical, statistical, and computational methods
Program some components of disease simulation models, including optimizing model for use with high-performance computing clusters and assisting with code debugging, maintenance, and documentation
Identify and assist in the development of methodological advances in the fields of disease simulation modeling, health decision science, and biostatistics
Supports design and analysis of population health datasets relevant to the field of diabetes and cardiometabolic diseases, including assembling preliminary data relevant to these themes.
Coordinates research study activities. Functions as a resource for study issues, particularly in relation to the creation and curation of large survey-based datasets. Identifies problems and helps to develop solutions.
Implements data quality and management activities, including data validation, quality control
Perform literature reviews and synthesize relevant data for these projects with other research staff; work with these staff to convert published data into appropriate model input parameters
Lead abstract and manuscript preparation, as well as presentation of study results for multiple projects at a time

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
About ATI:

Automated Tire (ATI) is on a mission to reinvent tire changing and wheel balancing using cutting-edge robotics. We are transforming a process that hasn't fundamentally changed in decades into an automated, high-performance system built for the future of automotive service.

Founded by experienced entrepreneurs and backed by leading players across the automotive and tire industries, ATI is building technology that will redefine how cars are serviced. Our team combines deep robotics expertise with real world deployment, moving fast from prototype to production and scaling solutions directly in the field.

If you are excited by hands-on robotics, real-world impact, and the challenge of building category-defining technology from the ground up, ATI is the place to do the most meaningful work of your career.

Position Overview:

We are looking for a Senior Data Analyst to join our growing robotics startup. In this role, you will sit at the intersection of data engineering and analytics — translating complex, high-dimensional data from robotics and sensor systems into actionable insights that drive product strategy and operational excellence. You will partner closely with software engineers, ML engineers, product managers, and hardware teams to ensure that data is not only available and reliable, but deeply understood and put to use.

Responsibilities:

Data Analysis & Business Intelligence


Serve as the primary analytical partner to business stakeholders — translating their questions and goals into metrics, frameworks, and insights that drive decisions across product, operations, and leadership
Translate complex, multi-source datasets (robotics telemetry, sensor streams, computer vision outputs) into actionable insights for both technical and non-technical audiences
Conduct analyses to identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities across the product stack — from edge device performance to model accuracy and fleet-level behavior
Define and track KPIs for product quality, model performance, data health, and operational efficiency
Design, build, and maintain interactive dashboards and reports (e.g., Tableau, Looker, Hex) that provide real-time visibility into business and product performance


Analytics Engineering


Define and own the analytical layer of the data and ML platform — building the data models, transformations, and pipelines that power reporting and insight delivery
Design, develop, and maintain self-serve analytics infrastructure: semantic layers, metrics catalogs, and reporting pipelines that empower the broader organization to answer their own questions
Partner with engineering to ensure dashboard data sources are reliable, performant, and aligned with defined data contracts and SLAs
Establish and document standards for the analytics layer, including naming conventions, metric definitions, refresh cadences, and access controls


Data Quality & Observability


Implement and champion data quality frameworks: automated validation, anomaly detection, SLA monitoring, and lineage tracking across all data sources
Implement robust observability: build dashboards, alerts, and metrics for pipeline performance, data drift, model health, and system reliability
Partner with engineering to define and enforce data contracts and schema standards across all upstream sources


Cross-Functional Collaboration & Leadership


Collaborate cross-functionally with product, hardware, and CV teams to align analytical decisions with product goals and user impact
Hire, mentor, and grow a world-class team of data and ML engineers; set technical standards and guide architectural decisions


Shape the strategic roadmap for how ATI leverages data and AI to unlock new capabilities in robotics and physical intelligence


Act as a data advocate — educating partners on best practices in data interpretation, statistical rigor, and decision-making under uncertainty


Requirements


8+ years of experience in data analytics, data science, or related roles — with demonstrable impact on product or operational outcomes
Expert-level SQL skills: complex query writing, CTEs, window functions, data modeling, schema design, partitioning, and query optimization across large-scale datasets — with the ability to independently source and validate data without engineering support
Experience with BI and visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Looker, Superset, or similar) and building self-serve analytics platforms
Experience with cloud infrastructure (AWS, GCP, Azure) and modern data ecosystems (data lakes, containers, serverless).
Excellent communication and collaboration skills; ability to translate technical constraints into product impact and convey complex findings to non-technical audiences


Preferred Qualifications:


Background in robotics, autonomous systems, SLAM, 3D perception, or sensor fusion
Familiarity with data lake architectures
Experience in closed-loop feedback systems, online learning, or real-time adaptation

Title: CRM Applications Analyst
Description:
Join a growing industrial manufacturing company where you won’t just manage a CRM — you’ll help build the processes, workflows, and foundation that drive long-term business growth. This is an opportunity to make a real impact, collaborate across teams, and grow your career alongside a company that values innovation, ownership, and continuous improvement.

Our benefits offering include:

“Work Today, get paid tomorrow” - withdraw up to 30% of your earned daily wages the next day after you work rather than waiting for pay day!
Chesterton Student Loan Repayment Program
BCBS Medical Coverage
Delta Dental
401K savings plan with a Chesterton contribution
PTO including vacation sick, time, 11 paid holidays, and paid to time off to volunteer for causes
Flexible work schedules, including fully remote and hybrid work options for many jobs
Vision insurance
Tuition Reimbursement
Short-term disability and long-term disability insurance
HSA & FSA options to cover additional medical costs
Parental leave for moms and dads to be
Hiring referral bonuses (up to $3,000 for select roles)




A host of other fringe benefits including legal services, pet insurance, Chesterton relief fund for employees in need, and more!

Job Summary:




The CRM Application Analyst owns the application for the organization. This role enables revenue growth and sales effectiveness by ensuring the CRM platform delivers clear pipeline visibility, accurate customer data, and actionable insights. This role aligns CRM capabilities with strategic priorities, enables consistent selling processes, and drives adoption and data quality to help sales and customer-facing teams execute more effectively and make better, faster decisions. Global role to coordinate with regions.




Job Model: Hybrid (2-3 days a week on site)




Hybrid positions will require employees to work both on-site and remote each week under a schedule that has been determined and agreed upon by you and your manager.

Responsibilities:




Consistent, regular and punctual attendance is an essential function of this job.
Serve as the business owner and subject matter expert for the CRM platform
Partner with Sales, Marketing, Engineering and Customer Service leadership to ensure CRM supports revenue growth, pipeline management, and customer engagement strategies
Translate commercial priorities into CRM improvements that enhance usability, consistency, insight, and decision making
Oversee CRM changes and enhancements to ensure alignment with business processes, data standards, and adoption goals
Champion CRM best practices, governance, and consistent usage across customer facing teams
Evaluate new CRM capabilities from a business value perspective and recommend adoption where they support revenue and efficiency objectives
Act as liaison between business stakeholders and IT for all CRM related initiatives
Gather, analyze, and document business requirements and translate them into functional CRM solutions
Document current-state CRM processes and design future state workflows to support sales effectiveness and operational efficiency
Collaborate with cross functional teams to align CRM capabilities with evolving business needs
Reporting, Data & Analytics
Design and maintain CRM dashboards, reports, and views that support pipeline visibility, forecasting, and performance tracking
Ensure CRM delivers accurate, timely, and actionable insights for sales leadership, marketing and customer facing teams
Monitor usage patterns and data trends to identify opportunities for improvement
Drive high data quality through governance, stewardship, and continuous improvement
Provide day to day CRM support to business users and act as a trusted advisor on best practices
Develop and deliver user training and documentation focused on consistent processes and value realization,
Support change management efforts related to CRM enhancements and new capabilities
Lead or participate in CRM user groups to gather feedback and drive continuous improvement
Partner with Sales leadership, Marketing leadership, Customer Service, and IT to support strategic initiatives
Ensure CRM usage aligns with company policies, data standards, and security requirements
Maintain confidentiality and responsible handling of customer and business data

Complexity of Duties:




Operates with minimal supervision as an independent contributor
Balances multiple priorities in a fast‑changing environment
Exercises sound judgment in complex or ambiguous business and data scenarios
Works effectively across functions and at multiple organizational levels

Essential Skills:




To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty satisfactorily. The requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or ability required. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.




Strong business analysis and problem‑solving skills
Ability to translate business needs into system‑enabled solutions
Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills
Strong analytical mindset with comfort using data to drive decisions
Proven ability to drive adoption, consistency, and continuous improvement

Education and/or Experience:




Bachelor’s degree or equivalent combination of other education and work experience.
4 - 6 years of relevant experience.
Experience with business requirements gathering, implementation, and training.
Strong analytical abilities, specifically related to understanding large data sets and managing process requirements.

Other Skills and Abilities:




CRM platform expertise
Business reporting and dashboarding
Data analysis, data governance, and data quality management
Understanding of system integrations and data relationships
Microsoft D365 a plus
Marketing Automation with Hubspot a plus

Physical Demands:




The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

While performing the duties of this job, the employee is occasionally required to stand, walk, sit, talk or hear and reach with hands and arms. Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision. Moderate noise (i.e.; computer printers, traffic) exists daily in the work environment.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Velir is an established mid-sized agency with a top-tier portfolio of clients, ranging from the world's largest non-profits to Fortune 500 brands. As of 2023, Velir acquired Brooklyn Data Company, a premier data and analytics consultancy focused on leadership, process improvement, implementation, and advanced analytics.

At Velir, we believe people are our greatest asset. Our culture is built on a foundation of trust, collaboration, and continued improvement. We strive for excellence in everything we do, embracing challenges as opportunities for growth. Our success is driven by a shared passion for making a positive impact on our customers, our communities and each other. We are a remote first company that offers competitive pay and excellent benefits.

Overview

Senior Data Analysts play a pivotal role in helping our client organizations extract valuable insights from their data to make informed decisions and drive business improvements. As senior-level individual contributors, responsibilities span from data strategy and data modeling to data analysis and visualization. Our senior data analysts work closely with clients to understand their specific business requirements to tailor data analytics solutions to meet those needs. This may also include recommending and implementing the right data analytics tools, platforms, and technologies for clients' needs.

Because our clients are mostly US-based organizations, we look for professional proficiency in English, verbally and in writing.

Responsibilities

Data Analytics Expertise: This role is responsible for leveraging structured data to identify patterns and use reports and visualizations to summarize them for strategic decision-making. You are capable of manipulating data through data cleaning, data conversion, and data modeling. As a senior member of the Analytics function, you serve as a mentor to other analysts, both individually and in group settings.


Uses SQL to write complex queries that join data across multiple systems (bonus points for proficiency in dbt!).
Derives key business metrics such as customer acquisition cost, customer lifetime value, monthly recurring revenue, or customer retention rate.
Collaborates and shares results with clients who have varying levels of data expertise.
Capable of designing, analyzing, and interpreting the results of experiments
Has strong knowledge of statistics to communicate significance, confidence, or probability.
Creates dashboards and visualizations using tools like Sigma, Omni, Domo, Looker, Tableau, Power BI.
Builds data models in dbt to support reporting and analysis
Mentors and helps others grow in the field
Helps our clients select, create, and report on key performance indicators with attention to user experience, and data storytelling


Cross-Team Collaboration: You are responsible for collaborating with peers and other functional departments to develop and implement analytics strategies and approaches that support engagement goals and understanding client needs.


Promotes a positive culture within and across different teams, collaborating with analytics engineers and data engineers on end-to-end client requirements
Collaborates with clients and functional managers to plan for analytics needs for a product or feature launch
Pairs with a teammate or with someone at a client on strategies for solving an analytical problem
Creates an analysis, process, or reporting template that helps cross-functional teams solve for common analytics problems
Regularly engages with other teams to make the organization more effective
Take initiative to identify and solve important problems. Coordinates with others on cross-cutting technical issues
Recommends improvements to tools, configurations, and workflows towards clients' goals and desired capabilities.


Project Delivery: You are responsible for contributing to the quality of larger and complex clients' deliverables as well as ensuring the on-time delivery of complex analytics projects.


Asks insightful questions to gather requirements and align expectations on what success looks like for a task
Digs into the root cause of reporting issues
Estimates accurate timelines for deliverables
Keeps reliability, maintainability, and scalability of our clients' systems top of mind
Helps assure quality of their own and team members' deliverables through quality assurance steps
Walks stakeholders through findings and recommendations
Embraces long-term ownership of projects while building shared knowledge for redundancy
Contributes to undesirable or unowned work that improves the quality or velocity of the team's efforts


Technical Skills


Programming: You have a practical understanding and background in analytics code and programming, as evidenced by the ability to maintain local development environments, contribute basic features (unit testing, following clear steps for completion), and write code thoughtfully to aid in testing and modularity. Proficiency in Python (nice to have).
Data Reporting: You are able to generate more complex static reports or dashboards independently, using advanced features and customization options.
Data Analysis: You have a thorough understanding of data analysis and can identify deviations from the norm and relationships between various data points.
Domain Expertise: You have a strong level of knowledge in domains in which you're working. You are able to relate how the business works with the goals of the immediate team.
Data Modeling & Transformation: You are familiar with how to convert, cleanse, and structure data into usable formats/structures to prepare it for analysis. You can effectively transform raw data into analysis-ready models for different use cases. You have some proficiency with data transformation tools such as dbt.
Data Science Techniques & Applications: You are familiar with how to identify, cleanse and automate the collection of valuable data. You can deploy techniques for quantitative data analysis of large data sets by building models and using machine-learning algorithms.


Qualifications


Proven experience as a Data Analyst or related role, with a focus on using data for business insights.
Strong programming skills in SQL. Experience with Python is a plus.
Hands-on experience with data visualizations tools like Looker, Tableau, Power BI, Sigma, Omni or Domo. (Certified in Sigma, Omni or Domo is a plus)
Proficiency with data modeling and transformation tools such as dbt
Proficiency with data science techniques and applications
Experience mentoring and advising other analysts. (Experience teaching BI tools is a plus)
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Excellent communication and collaboration skills.


Physical Requirements


Frequent sitting at a desk performing work on a computer
Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions


Compensation Range: $110,000 - 135,000 annually

Please note that compensation packages are finalized after the interview process is concluded. We use a competency-based approach to base pay, which means it is based on the competencies and skills demonstrated for this role.

Core Company Values


Take the Long View - Ensure the company is built to last
Be Courageous - Make the right decisions even when they aren't the easiest decisions
Be Genuine - Bring honesty and authenticity to all that you do
Work with Focus + Passion - Display purpose and pride in your work and never stop learning


As an equal opportunity employer, we are firmly committing to diversity, equity, and inclusion in our hiring efforts. We recognize that we need team members from all backgrounds and experiences to successfully shape a positive employee experience as well as deliver our product and service solutions. To that end, we actively seek candidates who can bring diverse experiences and backgrounds to our team. We know that complex factors and systemic bias can get in the way of us meeting strong candidates, so please don't hesitate to apply even if you're not 100% sure.

At this time, Velir does not sponsor candidates and unfortunately cannot accept those on OPT or CPT.

Title: Databricks Analyst
Description:
Databricks Analyst




Location: Remote (Boston, MA)




About the Role

We are seeking a detail-oriented Databricks Analyst to support data analytics and financial reporting initiatives. This role is ideal for someone with a strong finance or accounting background who has hands-on experience leveraging Databricks for data transformation, analysis, and reporting.

You will work cross-functionally with Finance, Accounting, and IT teams to build scalable data solutions, enhance reporting capabilities, and drive data-driven decision-making.

Key Responsibilities

Develop, maintain, and optimize data pipelines and analytical workflows in Databricks
Perform data extraction, transformation, and validation across financial systems
Build dashboards, reports, and financial models to support FP&A and accounting functions
Collaborate with stakeholders to translate business requirements into technical solutions
Ensure data accuracy, integrity, and governance standards are met
Support automation of financial reporting processes
Document processes, data flows, and technical solutions

Required Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, or related field
3–5 years of hands-on experience using Databricks
Strong understanding of financial statements, accounting principles, and reporting processes
Experience with SQL and data modeling
Experience working with large datasets and structured/unstructured data
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Excellent communication skills with the ability to work cross-functionally

Preferred Qualifications

Experience supporting FP&A, controllership, or audit functions
Exposure to cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP)
Experience integrating ERP or financial systems with Databricks
Familiarity with BI tools such as Power BI or Tableau

Title: Senior Transportation Data Analyst
Description:
Overview

ABOUT THE POSITION

Senior Transportation Data Analyst

Locations: Watertown, MA | Raleigh, NC | Orlando, FL | NYC | Washington DC | Atlanta, GA | Any additional office locations in VHB footprint

Are you a dedicated Senior Transportation Data Analyst looking to make a significant impact through cutting-edge technology and data-driven approaches? Join VHB's innovative Technology Enablement Team in a Senior Transportation Data Analyst position, where you'll work with a diverse team of engineers, scientists, planners, designers, and technologists. At VHB, we collaborate with government agencies to private businesses in transportation, real estate, institutional, and federal markets working collectively to enhance communities, stimulate economic growth, and promote environmental sustainability.

Step into a role where your analytical expertise and passion for innovation truly matter. At VHB, you’ll be empowered to drive meaningful change using data to uncover insights that shape smarter mobility, vibrant communities, and a sustainable future. This is more than just a job; it’s an opportunity to collaborate with top industry professionals and see your work make a real difference. Join VHB and turn your curiosity into community impact. Begin your rewarding journey with us today!

Please note: applicants must be legally authorized to work for VHB in the U.S. without employer sponsorship

Key Responsibilities


Engage in big data-related projects across the multiple services VHB provides to our diverse client base
Mine and analyze complex structured/unstructured data sets using highly advanced statistical methods to help with data-driven decision making
Develop and maintain dashboards and websites that support large databases, facilitate complex search queries, and enhance various business functions leveraging probe data
Develop innovative approaches to modify and build new data processes, and build large, complex data sets ensuring data integrity and statistical accuracy.
Design, develop, test, validate, and analyze models and advanced algorithms to extract optimal value from the data
Develop and apply customized algorithms or models to key business metrics with the goal of improving operations or answering business questions. Implement automated processes for efficiently producing scalable models
Interpret results, predict future data trends and other data foresights, and propose strategic business solutions
Train and mentor the data management team and other VHB subject matter experts on new or updated procedures


Skills And Attributes

Data Management & Analysis:


Proficiency in managing large datasets and understanding data structures.
Statistical analysis experience
Prescriptive and predictive analysis for operations, planning, and decision making


Technical Skills


Experience with tools and software such as Databricks, SQL, and data visualization platforms such as Power BI.
Working with large spatial and non-spatial datasets, API's, web services, cloud data platforms
Data manipulation skills using various scripting languages like Python or R.
Strong ETL process experience


Communication Skills


Demonstrated ability to articulate, both verbally and in written form, complex technical concepts clearly and effectively to both technical and non-technical stakeholders


Industry Knowledge


Experience with connected vehicle and cell phone data such as INRIX, RITIS, HERE, and TomTom
Awareness of industry-specific regulations and data privacy concerns


Certifications


Relevant certifications such as CDMP (Certified Data Management Professional) or equivalent
Databricks accreditation or certification


Minimum Qualifications


Minimum of 3+ years of experience with advanced analytics in the engineering/transportation industry specifically providing services to DOTs, transportation agencies and/or state and federal governments
Master’s degree in Data Science, Business Analytics, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or equivalent discipline
Applicants must be legally authorized to work for VHB in the U.S. without employer sponsorship


The base salary range for this position is $98,500 – 148,000 for the Washington DC, New York City and Massachusetts locations. This offer is determined based on a number of job-related factors including internal comparators, skills, education, training, credentials, experience, scope and complexity of role responsibilities and geographic location. In addition, VHB offers a holistic benefits package which can be found here .

Building What’s Next, Together.

Our people make the difference. We foster a technology-empowered, people‑driven, future‑ready culture where agility, innovation, and collaboration shape how we work every day.

When You Join VHB, You’ll Find


Meaningful work on complex projects with real community impact
A collaborative, inclusive culture that values curiosity and shared success
Support to grow your skills and lead through change
Learning and development supported by evolving tools and technologies, including AI
Best-in-class benefits and a flexible, hybrid work environment


We’re growing across the East Coast and are consistently recognized as a great place to work. Ready to shape what’s next? Learn more about what sets our employee experience apart and connect with us.

VHB is a proud Equal Opportunity Employer. Since our founding, we have intentionally fostered a culture of inclusion and belonging, supported by deep-rooted core values, one of which is diversity. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, or other characteristics protected by law.

Title: Healthcare Data Analyst (Clinic Administration)
Description:
The Healthcare Data Analyst will join a collaborative team at Joslin Diabetes Center supporting clinical operations, grounded in patient safety and improvement science to participate in projects that promote a culture of excellence.

The Healthcare Data Analyst will be responsible for acquisition, analysis, interpretation, and reporting of sensitive data to support quality and performance measurement and track improvement efforts. They must be able to work independently and collaboratively to locate and validate data, identify data requirements, and communicate data specifications to team members and leaders. They must be able to develop reporting/analytic solutions, but also be able to draw meaningful conclusions and make recommendations for actions.

The Healthcare Data Analyst will leverage software applications to design solutions and perform detailed data mapping and analysis. They will not only manage data and design new processes, but will be accountable for the timely and accurate production of routine monitoring reports and special projects. This requires experience working with databases, establishing data connections and running queries, aggregating and synthesizing data for business plans, trends, and projections, along with creativity, curiosity, and initiative to evolve the tools and reporting systems to meet the priorities of a dynamic healthcare environment. They will need an understanding of healthcare financial and operational processes, data management, as well as manipulation and interpretation of large databases.

This role is expected to have onsite training for the first few months then transition to a hybrid schedule.

As a health care organization, we have the responsibility to do everything in our power to care for and protect our patients, our colleagues and our communities. Beth Israel Lahey Health requires that all staff be vaccinated against influenza (flu) as a condition of employment.


Responsible for acquiring, validating, and querying data from data repositories and then using available tools to create dashboards, reports, and effectively synthesizing the data for the end user.
Works with members across teams to define problems and clarify objectives; determines data needs and assists in designing collection tools and reports. Evaluates data and reports to assess if reporting meets end user requirements.
Develops executive level presentations including key observations of statistical, financial and qualitative information. Provides consulting support in developing strategic recommendations to aid decision making by senior management.
Stays abreast of healthcare trends and market development as context for analyses, and becomes proficient with the health system's decision support and other systems.
Collaborates with Information Systems and enterprise data analytics team(s) to obtain and maintain sources and mapping tables.
Ensures data integrity and data quality, regularly reviews data for accuracy, identifies trends, and communicates changes.
Other duties as assigned.
Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Mathematics, Allied Health or related required. Master's degree in Public Health, Finance, Epidemiology, Biostatistics or equivalent preferred.
2-5 years related work experience.
Strong experience in data management and analytic tools and techniques; Strong experience in report writing, analytics, visualizations, dashboard creation; Strong experience demonstrating analytical, financial, qualitative and quantitative skills.
Advanced technical computer skills as required for technical support specific to functional area and related systems.



The pay range listed for this position is the annual base salary range the organization reasonably and in good faith expects to pay for this position at this time. Actual compensation is determined based on several factors, that may include seniority, education, training, relevant experience, relevant certifications, geography of work location, job responsibilities, or other applicable factors permissible by law.

  • Works with members across teams to define problems and clarify objectives; determines data needs and assists in designing collection tools and reports. Evaluates data and reports to assess if reporting meets end user requirements.
  • Develops executive level presentations including key observations of statistical, financial and qualitative information. Provides consulting support in developing strategic recommendations to aid decision making by senior management.
  • Stays abreast of healthcare trends and market development as context for analyses, and becomes proficient with the health system's decision support and other systems.
  • Collaborates with Information Systems and enterprise data analytics team(s) to obtain and maintain sources and mapping tables.

  • Other duties as assigned.
  • Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Mathematics, Allied Health or related required. Master's degree in Public Health, Finance, Epidemiology, Biostatistics or equivalent preferred.

  • Strong experience in data management and analytic tools and techniques; Strong experience in report writing, analytics, visualizations, dashboard creation; Strong experience demonstrating analytical, financial, qualitative and quantitative skills.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Who We Are:

Interra Health is a fast-growing healthcare technology company transforming how providers and patients navigate the prescription journey. Formed through the merger of DoseSpot, Arrive Health, and pVerify, Interra Health delivers trusted eligibility, real-time coverage and pricing insights, prescribing tools, and pharmacy transparency at the point of care—helping providers make informed decisions and patients access the right medications with greater clarity and affordability. Backed by strong market momentum and a bold vision for the future of connected care, Interra Health offers the chance to join an innovative, mission-driven team working at the intersection of software and healthcare to reduce friction, improve access, and make the healthcare experience better for everyone.

The Role:

As a Senior Data Analyst, you are responsible for collecting, analyzing, and visualizing data to generate actionable insights that inform business decisions. You design, build, and maintain dashboards and reporting frameworks that improve visibility into product performance, operational efficiency, and revenue outcomes. You help the team utilize AI capabilities to enhance our data platform.

You partner cross-functionally with Product, Operations, Finance, and Revenue teams to translate business needs into data solutions, strengthen reporting accuracy and accessibility, and proactively identify trends and opportunities that support strategic growth and continuous improvement.

Key Responsibilities:


Data Analysis & Reporting:
Collect, analyze, and interpret data from various sources, including our e-prescribing, real time prescription benefit (RTPB), eligibility, and electronic prior authorization (ePA) platforms.
Develop and maintain dashboards and reports that track key business metrics such as customer transactional volume.
Provide actionable insights and recommendations to internal stakeholders (product, operations, and revenue teams) to improve business workflows and performance.
Identify trends, bottlenecks, and areas for improvement; provide actionable insights for optimization.
Data Visualization:
Create interactive, user-friendly reports and dashboards that clearly present business-wide, or customer-specific, metrics to non-technical users.
Optimize and automate reports to ensure timely and consistent access to up-to-date information.
Collaboration with Stakeholders:
Work closely with product managers, engineers, and business leaders to understand business needs and translate them into data-driven solutions.
Partner with the data engineering team to inform data architecture, storage, and processing
Provide insights that support strategic decision-making for product development, operational improvements, and regulatory compliance.
Ad-Hoc Analysis & Research:
Perform ad-hoc analysis to support specific business initiatives, product development, or troubleshooting.
Research new trends and technologies within healthcare technology and the e-prescribing space to ensure the company remains at the forefront of innovation
Generative AI:
Help team utilize AI capabilities to advance our data platform and products
Documentation
Text to SQL
Automated Metric and Data Model Generation


What You’ll Bring:


4+ years of experience in data analysis, reporting, and data visualization
Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field preferred (Computer Science, Information Technology, Business Administration)
Demonstrated experience implementing reports or dashboards for stakeholders
Expertise in Power BI, Tableau, or an equivalent BI tool
Fluent with data querying and languages such as SQL (Snowflake)
Strong organizational and project management skills
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
Ability to collaborate cross-functionally but can work independently as needed
A data-driven approach to problem-solving and business decision-making.


Core Competencies:


Knowledge & Application: Complete knowledge and full understanding of area of specialization, principles and practices within a professional discipline. Assesses unusual circumstances and uses sophisticated analytical and problem solving techniques to identify cause. Resolves and assesses a wide range of issues in creative ways and suggests variations in approach.
Complexity & Problem Solving: Works on problems of diverse scope where analysis of information requires evaluation of identifiable factors. Devises solutions based on limited information and precedent and adapts existing approaches to resolve issues. Uses evaluation, judgment, and interpretation to select right course of action.
Collaboration & Interaction: Enhances relationships and networks with senior internal/ external partners who are not familiar with the subject matter often requiring persuasion. Adapts style to differing audiences and often advises others on difficult matters.


Working Conditions & Environment


Fully remote role within the United States
Periodic travel (approximately 5%) for team meetings
Operates in a fast-paced, growth-oriented, PE-backed SaaS environment
Requires cross-functional collaboration across Product, Sales, Customer Success, and Executive Leadership
Ability to manage multiple concurrent projects and shifting priorities
Success in this role requires the ability to balance synchronous collaboration with thoughtful asynchronous execution in a remote-first environment.


Benefits & Perks

🌍Remote work environment with a flexible work schedule to encourage work-life balance

✈Annual company offsite

🌴Generous leave package including flexible time off policy that encourages team members to take time off to relax and recharge; plus 13 paid holidays, paid sick leave, and paid parental leave

💙 Medical, dental, and vision insurance for you and your family, plus a company funded FSA & HSA (dependent on which medical plan you choose)

💰401(k) company match

💸One-time workspace reimbursement to help you optimize your remote workspace

Interra Health is an Equal Employment Opportunity/Affirmative Action employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, age, national origin, protected veteran status, disability status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, marital status, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by law.

Compensation Range: $100K - $130K

Description:

The compensation range for this full-time position applies to candidates located within the United States. Our salary ranges are determined by reviewing roles of similar responsibility and level. Within the salary range, individual pay is determined by several factors including performance, knowledge, job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Schneider Electric also offers a comprehensive benefits package to support our employees, inclusive of medical (with member reward points), dental, vision, and basic life insurance, Benefit Bucks (credits to apply towards your benefits) flexible work arrangements, paid family leaves, 401(k) + match, well-being and recognition (including service anniversary) programs, 12 holidays per year, 15 days of paid time off per year (pro-rated in the first year of employment based on start date), opportunity to purchase company stock (eligibility depends on start date), and military leave benefits.

You must submit an online application to be considered for the position. The Company will accept applications on an ongoing basis until the position is filled.

If you believe this job posting is not compliant with applicable state pay transparency laws in the U.S., please notify the Company as soon as possible upon discovery by completing this form Job Posting Compliance Form.

Key Responsibilities:


Gather and analyze data from various sources to identify trends and insights using ETL (Alteryx)
Develop and maintain interactive dashboards and visualizations using Tableau.
Collaborate with stakeholders to understand their data needs and provide actionable recommendations.
Conduct data validation and ensure accuracy and consistency in reporting in all production workflows and back-end infrastructure
Prepare and present reports to management and other stakeholders.
Assist in the design and implementation of data warehouses and data models.
Stay up to date with industry trends and emerging technologies in business intelligence.


Let us learn about you! Apply today.

Qualifications:


Bachelor’s degree in Business, Data Science, Computer Science, or a related field.
Proven experience as a Business Intelligence Analyst or similar role.
Proficiency in BI tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI, Databricks), SQL and ETL (Alteryx, etc.)
Proficiency in Microsoft suite (PowerApps, Power Automate, Excel, etc.)
Strong analytical skills and attention to detail.
Excellent communication and presentation skills.
Ability to work collaboratively in a team environment.
Agile experience a plus.

Title: HR Data Analyst, Talent Analytics
Description:
As a global medtech company, we are driven by our Vision of changing the trajectory of lives for a new day and our Mission to create ingenious solutions that ignite patient turnarounds. Our relentless commitment to patients and strong legacy of innovation in healthcare are the foundation of our future. If you're looking for a new chance, a new beginning, a new trajectory, LivaNova is where your talent can truly thrive. Join our talented team members worldwide to become a pioneer of tomorrow—because at LivaNova, we don’t just treat conditions — we aspire to alter the course of lives.

Position Summary

The HR Data Analyst, Talent Analytics will carry out overall strategy for employee data at LivaNova to support the need for data-driven decision-making regarding workforce productivity, engagement and better returns from recruitment and learning investments in the company.

Key Responsibilities


Compile, structure, and analyze data from HRIS, payroll and Finance systems to develop insights and conclusions that help streamline HR processes throughout the company.
Work with the full data analysis pipeline including data cleaning, process documentation, and communicating results via report writing and visualization.
Participate actively in HR Business Intelligence including project intake, visualization tools, quality assurance, and storytelling. Interpret employee data to identify significant differences, relationships, and trends in data, as well as factors that could affect the results of research.
Identify statistical analysis techniques required to deliver insights.
Advance the use of complex analytical techniques and statistical thinking.
Design and implement data models using statistical principles (e.g., regression, correlation) and develop predictive models for attrition, performance, and recruiting demand.
Produce concise, story-driven presentations to communicate analytic insights.
Collect and share information on government labor statistics and external benchmarks.
Continually analyze competitor practices to make appropriate recommendations to upper management.
Partner with HR leaders to design research plans, develop hypotheses, collect data, and run various statistical models.
Help recruiters by providing relevant data, statistics, trends, and patterns to make the recruiting process more efficient. Similar for talent development, total rewards and employee experience (e.g. retention) practices.
Collaborate with subject matter experts across the HR service areas (e.g. Talent Development, Total Rewards and Talent Acquisition), as well as HR leadership, to promote data governance and stewardship, and to improve overall strategic and operational performance and insight.
Work with the HR business partner for organizational development (e.g. organization levels, structure, teams, span of control) and partner with colleagues and customers to achieve meaningful discussions regarding data outputs and feedback on usage activities to improve HR output.
Educate the HR team on analytical findings, general data and data management best practices.
Interact with professional and community groups related to analytics and business intelligence and maintain strong internal and external networks of peers to stay always ahead of the curve.


Essential Knowledge & Experience


Master’s degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Business Analytics or closely related field
1 year of experience as a Human Resources Analyst or HR Data Analyst
The Master’s degree program must have included courses in Data Mining, Data-Driven Quality Management, Analytical Decision Modeling and provided technical hands-on experience with statistics, data analytics, data visualization (QLIK, Tableau or ggplot2), business intelligence, reporting and software experience with R or Python, a range of data types of various structures and sizes, and an understanding of the differences and use cases for interpretable vs. black box models.


Pay Transparency: A reasonable estimate of the annual base salary for this position is $90,000 - $106,000 + discretionary annual bonus. Pay ranges may vary by location.

Employee Benefits Include


Health benefits – Medical, Dental, Vision
Personal and Vacation Time
Retirement & Savings Plan (401K)
Employee Stock Purchase Plan
Training & Education Assistance
Bonus Referral Program
Service Awards
Employee Recognition Program
Flexible Work Schedules


Welcome to impact. Welcome to innovation. Welcome to your new life.

Title: Field Data Collector - Boston, MA - no experience needed
Description:
Short-term, part-time, flexible working schedule

Join our team as a Field Data Collector! In this role, you will conduct parking data collection surveys at various locations within the city, taking photos and entering notes using the mobile app. Your observations will be used to test and improve parking data. The work will be carried out within the city of Boston, MA, United States. Payment is $1.50 per block for the dynamic collection; $2.50 per submission for the testing collection.

Collect all relevant parking data at the test site, curbside and/or parking lots
Take photos of all unique parking signs and any other indicators on block/lot that relate to parking

Notate data errors in the mobile application when observed
Submit data, including photos and notes, using the mobile application
Ensure accurate and timely data collection

A recent and capable smartphone model with a data plan
Attention to detail and sharp observation skills
Ability to walk for extended periods
Computer literacy
The ability to work with maps and navigate terrain is a plus

Title: Senior Associate - Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

We are currently seeking a senior associate data analyst to help us collect, transform and extract meaning from data for the Global Technology Services (GTS) Control Tower team at State Street. The position performs a wide range of activities with experts from GTS, business intelligence, business analysis, data engineering, process engineering, governance, and data analysts to collaboratively solve a wide array of complex technical problems.

About The GTS Control Tower Team

The GTS Control Tower team is an exciting and growing area of State Street with unique insight into the whole spectrum of our data, metrics, and dashboards at State Street. This name change reflects the work we are actively doing across GTS: integrating dependency management, business analysis, process design, and governance to transform data into action and keep remediation efforts flying smoothly.

 

The GTS Control Tower Mission

Our core focus is on transforming data into actionable insights. Our goal is to enable GTS Leadership to accelerate progress against key strategic initiatives across GTS and to drive results.  

Core Responsibilities


Report & Dashboard Development: Design, develop, and maintain high‑quality, accessible interactive Power BI dashboards and reports that clearly communicate key performance indicators (KPIs) and business insights.
Data Modeling & Transformation: Build and optimize semantic data models, including relationships, calculated columns, and measures using DAX. Perform data extraction, transformation, and loading (ETL) using Python and Power Query (M).
Data Source Integration: Connect, consolidate, and manage data from multiple sources such as SQL databases, Azure services, Excel, and other enterprise systems into reliable, well‑structured datasets.
